mealprep::garlic alfredo steak mashed po.name: Garlic Alfredo Steak & Mashed Potatoes
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Topside/ Top Sirloin Beef, 1 inch cubed
Pinch of Chilli Flakes (omit for no spice)
1 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped Mashed Potatoes
1/2 Tsp Chilli Flakes (omit for no spice)
steps: Slice the beef steaks into 1-inch cubes. Season generously with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, parsley, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until the steak bites are evenly coated, then set aside to marinate while you prep the potatoes.

Wash, peel, and chop the potatoes into thick, even chunks. Place them in a pot of cold water, bring to a boil, and cook for 12–14 minutes until just fork- tender. Drain the water, then cover the pot with a lid and let the potatoes steam for 5 minutes— this helps soften them further for a smooth mash.

Use a potato masher to mash the steamed potatoes gently until smooth and fluffy. Avoid over-mashing to keep the texture light. Add a knob of butter, cream cheese, milk, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix gently until fully combined and creamy.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add butter, and sear the marinated steak bites for 2–3 minutes on each side until golden brown and crusted. Lower the heat, then add more butter, minced garlic, chilli flakes, and chopped parsley. Toss the steak bites in the buttery glaze until coated and aromatic, then set aside

In a blender, combine cottage cheese and milk. Blend for 2 minutes until smooth. Back in the same pan over low heat, melt a small amount of butter. Add smoked paprika, chilli flakes, salt, mustard, plenty of minced garlic, and parsley. Sauté for 5 minutes until fragrant. Pour in the blended cottage cheese and stir until the Alfredo sauce becomes creamy and slightly thick. Remove from heat.
To serve, portion the creamy mash and steak bites into 5 meal prep containers. Drizzle generously with the homemade Alfredo sauce. Your flavour-packed highprotein meals are ready to ENJOY! 51

mealprep::chicken shawarma rice bowls.name: chicken Shawarma Rice Bowls
ingredients: 40g Light Butter (for cooking all pieces) Yogurt Mix
1 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped Samurai Sauce
30g (1.1oz) Buffalo Hot Sauce (or any hot sauce)
½ Tsp each: Salt, Black Pepper, Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Paprika
steps: Butterfly slice the chicken breasts into thinner fillet halves. Season both sides with salt, black pepper, onion powder, parsley, smoked paprika, cumin, minced garlic, and lemon juice. Rub the spices in thoroughly, ensuring even coverage, then set aside to marinate.

For the golden rice, heat a pot over medium heat and melt butter. Add the spice blend (turmeric, paprika, salt, cumin) and toast for 1–2 minutes until fragrant. Stir in the washed basmati rice, mixing until each grain is coated in the spices. Pour in 400g water (double the rice weight), bring to a light boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for 7–8 minutes until fully cooked and fluffy. Drain any excess water and set aside.

While the rice cooks, prepare the samurai sauce and cucumber salad. Samurai Sauce: In a bowl, whisk together yogurt, mayo, hot sauce, lemon juice, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika until smooth. Store in a squeeze bottle for easy use. Cucumber Salad: In another bowl, combine yogurt, chopped cucumbers, tomatoes, and fresh parsley. Season with salt, garlic powder, and cumin to taste. Mix well and store separately for easier reheating.

Heat a pan over medium heat and melt butter. Cook the chicken fillets for 3–4 minutes per side, ensuring a golden-brown crust without burning the spices. Set aside to rest and repeat until all chicken is cooked.
Once everything is ready, slice the chicken into bite-sized cubes. Serve with golden rice, cucumber salad, and a generous drizzle of samurai sauce. Garnish with fresh parsley and ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken fajita mac n cheese.name: chicken Fajita Mac n cheese
ingredients: 2 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped Veggie Mix for Chicken/Half for Mac)
30g (2 Tbsp) Minced Garlic Fajita Mac n Cheese
steps: Slice boneless, skinless chicken breasts into bite- size cubes and place them in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, oregano, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, and a drizzle of olive oil. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is evenly coated and takes on a deep reddish hue. Set aside to marinate for at least 20–30 minutes.

Bring a pot of water to a boil and cook the macaroni pasta for 8–10 minutes, or until al dente. Once cooked, reserve a little pasta water (optional), drain the rest, and set the pasta aside for later.

Heat a large pan over medium-high heat, melt a little butter, then add finely chopped onions and red, yellow, and green bell peppers. Cook for 5–6 minutes until softened and fragrant. Add a generous amount of fajita seasoning and minced garlic, and continue cooking for another 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the veggies are golden and slightly caramelised. Transfer this fajita veggie mix to a bowl and set aside.

In the same pan, melt a bit more butter and cook the marinated chicken on medium heat for 5 minutes per side, or until golden brown and seared. Lower the heat, then add a few spoonfuls of the fajita veggie mix, a drizzle of honey, and fresh chopped parsley. Toss the chicken until well coated in the sweet, savoury mixture. Set aside and repeat if cooking in batches.

To the same pan on medium-low heat, return the remaining fajita veggie mix. Pour in the blended cheese sauce (made from milk, cream cheese, and cottage cheese, blended until smooth). Stir continuously until the sauce is warm and velvety, then add low-fat cheddar cheese. Let it melt and thicken slightly, stirring as needed.

Add your cooked macaroni to the pan and gently fold it through the sauce until every piece is coated and the mac is ultra creamy. Let it simmer for a minute if needed to reach your preferred consistency.

Serve into 5 meal prep containers, topping each with the juicy fajita chicken. Garnish with fresh parsley or a sprinkle of cheese (optional), and your high- protein Chicken Fajita Mac N Cheese is ready to ENJOY all week long! 55

mealprep::orange chicken rice bowls.name: Orange chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, cubed
250g (8.8oz) Uncooked White Rice/690g Cooked (leftover or day old cooked)
Garnish Sesame Seeds Salad (Makes a large batch)
1 or 2 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes (omit for no spice)
steps: Slice the boneless, skinless chicken thighs into bite-size cubes and place them in a mixing bowl. Season with black pepper, garlic and ginger paste, red pepper flakes, honey, sriracha, freshly squeezed orange juice, orange zest, and dark soy sauce. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is fully coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for best flavour).

Heat a large pan on medium heat, melt a small amount of butter, then add sesame seeds. Toast for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until golden and nutty. Add minced garlic and cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant. Season lightly with salt, then add leftover cooked rice and stir well. Cook for 2–3 minutes until fluffy, toasted, and aromatic. Garnish with chopped green onion and more sesame seeds, then set aside.

While the rice cooks, finely slice your vegetables: cucumber, red onion, carrots, cabbage, and green onion. Place in a large bowl and add rice vinegar, red pepper flakes, and a light drizzle of honey. Toss everything together until well combined. Store this crunchy slaw in a separate airtight container in the fridge - it stays crisp and tangy throughout the week.

Line a baking tray with baking paper, then evenly spread out the marinated chicken. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 19–22 minutes until golden brown and slightly charred on the edges. Let it rest for a few minutes before serving with all its juices.

Assemble your meal prep: divide the sesame garlic rice, orange-glazed chicken, and crunchy slaw into 5 meal prep containers. Keep the slaw separate if reheating. Your high-protein orange chicken bowls are ready to ENJOY all week! 57

mealprep::creamy cajun steak parmesan po.name: creamy cajun Steak & Parmesan Potatoes
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, 1 inch cubed
1 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped Crusted Potatoes
steps: Slice the beef sirloin steaks into 1-inch cubes, then place them in a large mixing bowl. Season with salt, Italian herbs, chilli flakes, garlic powder, onion powder, parsley, smoked paprika, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until each piece is evenly coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate while you prep the veg.

Slice the washed, uncooked potatoes into small cubes. Place them on a large tray along with chopped red onion, broccoli florets, and red bell peppers. Season generously with Italian herbs, garlic powder, paprika, parsley, and grated parmesan. Use clean hands to toss everything thoroughly until all the vegetables are well coated. Add more seasoning if needed.

Transfer the seasoned vegetables onto a lined baking tray. Oven bake or air fry at 180°C (360°F) for 20–24 minutes, or until the potatoes and vegetables are tender, golden, and lightly crisped on the edges. Once done, set aside.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat, melt a little butter, then add the marinated steak bites in a single layer. Cook for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and seared. Avoid overcrowding the pan - work in batches if needed. Once cooked, lower the heat and add minced garlic and fresh parsley, stirring until the beef is coated in a garlicky glaze. Set aside to rest.

In the same pan over medium-low heat, melt a bit more butter and toast the extra seasonings for 1–2 minutes until aromatic. Then pour in milk, followed by cream cheese and grated parmesan. Stir continuously until the sauce becomes smooth, melted, and creamy.

Let the sauce simmer gently for 2–3 minutes until it thickens slightly. Then assemble your meals: divide the roasted parmesan potatoes and veggies evenly into 5 portions, top with the juicy steak bites, and drizzle with the creamy sauce. Garnish with extra parsley if desired—and your high-protein meal prep is ready to ENJOY all week! 59

mealprep::caramelized onion creamy chick.name: caramelized Onion creamy chicken
ingredients: 10g (2 Tsp) Reduced Fat Butter (for cooking) Onion Creamy Sauce
350g (12.4oz) Red or White Onion, finely sliced
1 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped Butter Herb Rice
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into bite-sized cubes and transfer to a mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, oregano, smoked paprika, dried basil, and a drizzle of olive oil. Mix thoroughly until each piece is evenly coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate.

Heat a large pan over medium heat, melt down a little butter, then add the chicken in a single layer. Sear for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and slightly charred. Work in batches if needed. Once cooked, set aside to rest.

In the same pan, reduce heat to medium-low. Add more butter and toss in all the sliced onions. Cook slowly for 15 minutes or longer, stirring occasionally until deep golden and caramelised—this step builds flavour, so take your time. Meanwhile, boil and cook your white basmati rice in a separate pot for later use. Once onions are caramelised, season with salt, black pepper, paprika, and chilli flakes. Then add minced garlic and sun-dried tomatoes, cooking for an additional 2 minutes until fragrant..

Lower the heat fully and pour in milk, followed by cream cheese and grated parmesan. Stir gently and continuously until the mixture is smooth, creamy, and well combined. Now return the seared chicken to the pan along with a handful of freshly chopped parsley, giving everything a final stir until the chicken is coated in the creamy, savoury sauce.

For the rice, use a clean pan or the same one if large enough. On medium heat, melt a small amount of butter, then sauté minced garlic for 2 minutes until aromatic. Add salt to taste and a generous pinch of mixed or Italian herbs. Toast the herbs briefly for 30 seconds to release their oils, then add in the cooked rice.

Mix the rice well so the flavours fully combine. Remove from the heat, then grate over some fresh lemon zest and add a squeeze of lemon juice. Stir one last time until the rice is light, fragrant, and herby.
Evenly portion the lemon butter herb rice and creamy chicken into 5 meal prep containers. Garnish with more parsley if desired, and your high- protein meals are ready to ENJOY all week long!

mealprep::sweet sour chicken fried rice.name: Sweet & Sour chicken Fried Rice
ingredients: Garnish Sesame Seeds & Red Pepper Flakes
steps: Slice boneless, skinless chicken thighs into bite- sized cubes and place them into a mixing bowl. Season with black pepper, onion powder, minced garlic, ketchup, pineapple juice, rice vinegar, and soy sauce. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is fully coated and vibrant in colour. Set aside to marinate while prepping the rest.

If you’re not using leftover rice, start by boiling a pot of water. Add raw white rice and cook for 8–10 minutes until fluffy and fully cooked. Drain if needed and set aside to cool (cooling prevents clumping later). Meanwhile, dice your fresh pineapple (or drain canned pineapple), and chop red onion and red, yellow, and green bell peppers into small cubes.

Spread the marinated chicken evenly on a lined sheet pan, followed by the chopped pineapple and vegetables. Arrange in a single layer to ensure even cooking—don’t overcrowd the tray. If needed, bake in batches. Note: Bell peppers and pineapple will release liquid during baking, so avoid overloading. Drizzle honey lightly across the top for extra glaze and sweetness.

Oven bake at 200°C (400°F) for 30 minutes until the chicken is golden and tender. If using an air fryer, cook at 200°C for 25–30 minutes. If the chicken hasn’t browned to your liking, broil for an extra 3–5 minutes, keeping a close eye to avoid burning. The juices in the tray should reduce and create a sticky, caramelised glaze.

Heat a large pan over medium-high heat, melt some butter, then sauté chopped garlic, the white part of green onion, and diced carrots. Cook for 5–6 minutes until soft and fragrant. Push the veggies to one side of the pan.

On the empty side, pour in beaten eggs and stir continuously until fully scrambled. Mix the eggs together with the sautéed vegetables. Add in the leftover cooked rice, a splash of soy sauce, chopped kimchi, and kimchi juice. Finish with the green part of the green onion and stir everything together until well incorporated, fluffy, and full of colour.

Garnish with sesame seeds and red pepper flakes, then distribute the kimchi fried rice and sweet & sour chicken evenly into 5 meal prep containers. Your high-protein, flavour-packed meals are ready to ENJOY all week! 63

mealprep::chipotle beef potato bowls.name: chipotle Beef Potato Bowls
ingredients: Splash of Water (if too dry) Crispy Potatoes
1.5 Tsp Chipotle Chilli Flakes (Omit for less heat)
3 Tsp Olive Oil Chipotle Sauce (enough for 8+ meals)
250g (8.8oz) Low Fat Greek Yogurt (or yogurt of choice)
1/2 Tsp each: Salt, Garlic, Paprika Pico De Gallo (enough for 6+ meals)
steps: Slice the potatoes into small cubes. Season with garlic powder, onion powder, regular or chipotle chilli flakes, parsley, smoked paprika, cumin, and a drizzle of olive oil. Use your hands to mix well until every piece is evenly coated.
Spread the seasoned potatoes on a lined sheet pan and oven bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20- 23 minutes until golden and crispy. Once cooked, sprinkle salt to taste.

While the potatoes cook, prepare the creamy chipotle sauce. In a blender, combine low-fat yogurt, light mayo, chipotle paste, honey, lime juice, salt, garlic powder, and paprika. Blend until smooth, then bottle and refrigerate to use throughout the week.

Heat a pan over high heat, melt butter, and cook the ground beef for 6-8 minutes until browned and fully cooked. Lower the heat to medium, then add salt, garlic powder, onion powder, parsley, paprika, cumin, and chipotle paste. Mix thoroughly. If too dry, add a splash of water, then finish with a drizzle of honey and cook until juicy and golden. Remove from heat.

For the salsa, combine chopped tomatoes, red onion, red and green bell peppers, coriander, salt, pepper, and lime juice in a large bowl. Mix well and store separately to keep fresh and make reheating easier.
Serve into 5 even portions. Store in the fridge and reheat in a microwave or pan when ready to enjoy! 65

mealprep::harissa chicken rice bowls.name: Harissa chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 160g (5.6oz) Harissa Paste or Red Pepper Paste
Splash of Pickle Juice (for desired consistency) Cucumber Salad
300g (10.6oz) Fresh Tomatoes, de seeded & finely chopped
steps: In a blender or food processor, add roughly chopped tomatoes, a handful of parsley, garlic cloves, onion, red bell pepper, yogurt, harissa paste, salt, smoked paprika, oregano, cumin, and a drizzle of olive oil. Blend in short pulses for 5-8 seconds until slightly chunky - you want texture, not a completely smooth paste.
Add the marinade to the boneless chicken thighs and mix thoroughly with your hands until fully coated. Set aside to marinate while you prepare the rice.

Boil uncooked basmati rice in a pot for 8–10 minutes until fluffy. Meanwhile, heat a pan over medium heat, melt butter, and sauté chopped onions for 5 minutes until soft. Add salt, turmeric, parsley, paprika, and cumin. Toast for 2 minutes, then stir in the cooked rice until it turns golden and fluffy. Set aside.
Spread the marinated chicken evenly on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20-25 minutes until golden and juicy. Broil for 2-3 minutes at the end if needed for extra char.

While the chicken cooks, make the creamy harissa sauce: blend low-fat yogurt, light mayo, harissa paste, and a splash of pickle juice until smooth. Bottle and refrigerate. For the salad, combine chopped tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, and parsley in a large bowl. Store separately to keep fresh and make reheating easier.
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for 2–3 minutes, then chop into bite-size pieces. Serve into 5 portions with the golden rice, salad, and creamy harissa sauce. Enjoy your high-protein meals! 67

mealprep::honey butter chicken alfredo p.name: Honey Butter chicken Alfredo Pasta
ingredients: 2 Pieces Parmesan Rind layer, adds so much flavour!)
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into small cubes and place in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, chilli flakes, parsley, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, and a drizzle of olive oil. Mix thoroughly with your hands until the colour deepens and the chicken is evenly coated. Set aside to marinate.
Bring a pot of water to a boil and add your pasta of choice - linguine works great. Cook for 8 minutes or until al dente. Drain the pasta and set it aside.

Heat a pan on medium heat and melt some butter. Cook the chicken cubes for 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Once cooked, lower the heat and add a little more butter, a drizzle of honey, soy sauce, and fresh parsley. Mix everything well until the chicken is fully glazed and coated in the sauce. Set aside and repeat for the remaining chicken if needed.

In the same pan on medium-low heat, add lots of minced garlic and chopped sun-dried tomatoes. Cook for 3 minutes until soft and fragrant. Then add Italian herbs, salt, low-fat milk, light cream cheese, grated parmesan, and a couple of parmesan rind pieces (optional for depth of flavour). Keep stirring gently until the sauce is completely smooth and slightly thickened.

Add the cooked pasta along with more fresh parsley. Gently mix everything together until the sauce coats each strand and the pasta is creamy and indulgent. z F I N A L S T E P Serve and distribute evenly into 5 meal prep containers with the honey butter chicken. Garnish if desired - your high-protein meals are ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::philly cheesesteak rice bowls.name: Philly cheesesteak Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Top Sirloin Beef, sliced into thin strips
260g (9oz) Uncooked Basmati Rice/ Approx 600g Cooked Rice
steps: Slice the beef steaks into extra thin strips and place in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, Worcestershire sauce, and olive oil. Mix well with your hands until evenly coated, then set aside to marinate.

Heat a pan over medium heat and melt some butter. Add sliced onions and mixed bell peppers, then cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and fragrant. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper, mix well, then set aside.

In the same pan, melt a little more butter on medium heat. Sauté minced garlic for 30 seconds, then add in your leftover cooked rice along with chilli flakes, plenty of grated parmesan, and freshly chopped parsley. Mix everything until fluffy, golden, and aromatic. Set aside once done.

Bring the heat up to medium-high, then add the marinated steak strips in a single layer. Sear for 4-6 minutes until browned and slightly charred. Once cooked, add the sautéed onions and peppers back in. Then add light cheese slices, a splash of milk, and low-fat cheddar cheese. Mix thoroughly until the cheese is melted, creamy, and well combined with the steak and veggies.
Serve and divide the garlic parmesan rice and Philly cheesy steak mix into 5 equal portions. Your high-protein meals are ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::crispy nashville hot chicken m.name: crispy Nashville Hot chicken Mac n cheese
ingredients: 1 Tsp Each: Smoked Paprika, Garlic & Onion Powder
1/2 Tsp each: Black Pepper, Chilli Powder
steps: Slice the boneless chicken thighs into cubes and place in a mixing bowl. Season with black pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, chilli powder, baking powder, dark brown sugar, and a drizzle of olive oil. Mix thoroughly with your hands until well coated, then set aside to marinate.
Bring a pot of water to a boil, add the uncooked macaroni, and cook for 8 minutes until al dente. Drain and set aside for later.

Heat a pan on medium heat, melt some butter, then cook the marinated chicken for 5 minutes on each side (don’t over crowd the pan) until golden brown with a crispy crust. Lower the heat, add a little more butter, a dry spice mix (smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder), and fresh parsley. Stir until the chicken is juicy and well coated, then set aside. Repeat for any remaining chicken.

Using the same pan on low heat, add low fat milk, light cream cheese, low fat cheddar, dijon mustard, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, chilli powder, and chopped parsley. Stir continuously until the sauce is smooth, melted, and creamy.

Add the cooked macaroni to the sauce and gently fold until evenly combined and creamy. Divide the mac and cheese with the crispy Nashville chicken into 5 portions - your high protein meals are ready to ENJOY! 73

mealprep::cajun honey butter chicken cre.name: cajun Honey Butter chicken & creamy Potatoes
ingredients: Extra Seasoning: garlic, Onion Powder, Paprika, Oregano
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes and place in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, oregano, chilli flakes, and a drizzle of olive oil. Mix well until fully coated and the colour deepens, then set aside to marinate.

Cut the uncooked potatoes into small cubes and place in a separate bowl. Season with garlic powder, Italian herbs, paprika, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly, then lay them flat on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20-22 minutes until golden and crispy.

While the potatoes cook, heat a pan on medium, melt a little butter, and cook the seasoned chicken for 6-8 minutes until golden. Lower the heat, then add extra butter, this seasoning mix (garlic & onion powder, paprika, oregano), a drizzle of honey, and fresh parsley. Mix well until fully glazed and juicy, then set aside. Repeat if needed for remaining chicken.

In the same pan, add chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until soft. Stir in tomato paste and cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant. Lower the heat, then pour in evaporated milk and add garlic & herb cream cheese. Stir continuously until the sauce is smooth and slightly thickened.

Once the potatoes are cooked, fold them into the creamy sauce gently until fully coated and indulgent. Divide the creamy potatoes and honey butter chicken into 5 even portions - your high-protein meals are ready to ENJOY! 75

mealprep::street corn chicken bowls.name: Street corn chicken Bowls
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Chicken Breast, cut into cubes
2 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, finely chopped Corn Salsa
50g (1.7oz) Cholula Hot Sauce, or hot sauce of choice
50g (1.7oz) Jalapeños, de seeded and finely chopped
Optional Squeeze of Lime, i didn’t add it as the other components already have lime juice
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes and place them in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cumin, parsley, olive oil, and a good squeeze of lime juice. Toss until evenly coated and set aside to marinate while you prep the rice.

In a pot, add water using a 1:2 rice-to-water ratio. Add the washed uncooked rice, bring to a light boil, then reduce to a simmer for 10–12 minutes or until almost fully cooked. Once slightly underdone, remove from heat, drain any excess water, and cover with a lid to let the rice steam and fluff up. Stir in a big handful of chopped fresh coriander and a pinch of salt. Mix gently to combine.

Heat a pan over medium heat and melt some butter. Add the marinated chicken and cook for 4–5 minutes per side until golden and seared. Lower the heat, then stir in extra butter, minced garlic, and chopped fresh parsley. Toss the chicken to coat it in the buttery, fragrant glaze.

In a large bowl, combine sweetcorn, light mayo, Greek yogurt, chopped and deseeded jalapeños, fresh coriander, Cholula hot sauce, Tajín, lime juice, and crumbled feta. Mix until creamy and well combined. Store in a separate container for best freshness.
Divide the chicken, coriander rice, and creamy corn salad into 5 equal portions. Your high-protein meals are now ready to fuel your week!

mealprep::chilli lime chicken potato bow.name: chilli Lime chicken Potato Bowls
ingredients: Marinate for as long as you can
Spread evenly on a lined sheet pan, make sure it fills the pan well
Oven bake or air fry for 20- 22 mins at 200C / 400F Crusted Crispy Potatoes
3 Tsp Olive Oil Verde Creamy Sauce
Pinch of Salt & Pepper Mango Salsa
150g (5.3oz) Red & Green Bell Peppers, finely chopped
steps: Trim excess fat from the chicken thighs and place them in a large mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, lots of minced garlic, chilli flakes, smoked paprika, a drizzle of honey, lime zest and juice, and a splash of light soy sauce. Mix thoroughly with your hands until well coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate.

Slice the uncooked potatoes into small cubes and place on a lined sheet pan. Season with black pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, parsley, garlic powder, shredded coconut, and olive oil. Toss well until evenly coated, then spread out on the tray.Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 20–24 minutes until golden, crispy, and aromatic. Sprinkle with salt once cooked and set aside.
In a blender, combine Greek yogurt, light mayo, honey, 1 deseeded green chilli, a large handful of fresh coriander, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth, then bottle and refrigerate.

In a large bowl, add finely chopped cucumber (deseeded), red onion, ripe mango, red and green bell peppers, and fresh coriander. Mix until well combined. Optionally, sprinkle chilli flakes or Tajín seasoning. Store in a separate container and refrigerate.
Spread the marinated chicken thighs evenly on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 20–22 minutes until juicy and lightly charred.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before slicing into cubes. Distribute the chicken, potatoes, sauce, and salad into 5 portions - and your high protein meals are ready for the week!

mealprep::creamy garlic butter steak bit.name: creamy Garlic Butter Steak Bites
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Top Sirloin Steaks, cut into 1 inch cubes
250g (8.8oz) Low Fat Milk or Light Evaporated Milk
steps: Slice the beef steaks into 1-inch cubes and place them in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, Italian herbs, garlic powder, paprika, and a drizzle of olive oil. Mix thoroughly with your hands until evenly coated, then set aside to marinate.

In a large pan over low heat, melt a little butter or spray lightly with oil. Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and a sprinkle of Italian herbs. Sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant. Add the cooked white rice and mix well until everything is incorporated. Remove from heat, stir in plenty of grated parmesan and freshly chopped parsley, then fluff until rich, cheesy, and aromatic.

Heat a separate large pan over medium heat and melt a little butter. Add the marinated steak bites and sear for 3–4 minutes on each side until browned and juicy. Reduce the heat, then add a bit more butter, minced garlic, and chopped parsley. Toss until the beef is coated in the garlicky butter glaze. Cook in batches if needed to avoid overcrowding.

Using the same pan on low heat, add low-fat milk, light cream cheese, paprika, and mixed herbs. Stir continuously until the cream cheese is fully melted and the sauce is smooth. Let it bubble gently to thicken to your desired consistency. Store separately for the best texture and taste when reheating.
Distribute the Tuscan-style rice and steak bites evenly into 5 meal prep containers. Your high-protein meals are now ready to enjoy throughout the week!

mealprep::korean bbq chicken mac n chees.name: Korean BBQ chicken Mac n cheese
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, fat trimmed
1.5 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes or Chilli Flakes
120g (4.2ox) Low Fat Cheddar Cheese Cucumber Salad
steps: Trim excess fat from the boneless chicken thighs and place them in a large mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, red pepper flakes, and olive oil. Mix until evenly coated, then set aside to marinate.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add macaroni or your preferred pasta and cook for 8-10 minutes until al dente. Drain and set aside.

In a mixing bowl, combine thinly sliced cucumbers, red onion, green onion, red pepper flakes, garlic powder, sesame seeds, gochujang sauce, light soy sauce, and rice vinegar. Mix thoroughly, then store in a separate container and refrigerate.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and melt a little butter. Cook the marinated chicken thighs for 4-5 minutes on each side until golden and crispy. Remove from the pan and set aside.

In the same pan over low heat, add minced garlic, gochujang hot sauce, a drizzle of honey, and rice vinegar. Stir to form a rich, vibrant glaze. Add the chicken back to the pan and coat evenly until glossy and golden. Remove from heat and set aside.

In the same pan over medium-low heat, add milk, cream cheese, salt, black pepper, paprika, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stir until smooth, then add low- fat cheddar and mix until melted. Add the cooked macaroni and stir until fully coated and creamy.
Slice the glazed Korean chicken into bite-sized cubes. Distribute evenly across 5 servings along with the mac & cheese and cucumber gochujang salad. Your high-protein meals are ready to ENJOY! 83

mealprep::korean bbq beef bowls.name: Korean BBQ Beef Bowls
ingredients: Milk/Evaporated Milk for desired consistency Grain Rice
steps: In a bowl, combine light and dark soy sauce, garlic and ginger paste, gochujang, honey, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame seeds. Mix until fully combined, then set aside.

Heat a pan on medium, melt some butter, and cook chopped onions for 5 minutes until soft. Increase heat to high, add lean ground beef, and cook for 8 minutes until browned, using a spatula to break it up.
Lower the heat to medium, pour in the prepared sauce, and cook for 4-5 minutes until the beef turns golden and slightly caramelised. Finish with chopped green onion and sesame seeds, then set aside.
For the Korean spicy sauce, mix low-fat yogurt, gochujang, and honey in a bowl. Add a splash of milk to loosen the consistency if needed. Bottle and store for future use.

In a large bowl, combine thinly sliced cucumber, grated carrots, red onion, green onion, light soy sauce, sriracha, rice vinegar, and garlic powder. Toss gently until evenly coated. Store separately to keep fresh and make reheating easier.
Serve and divide the Korean beef, white rice, crunchy slaw, and spicy sauce into 5 portions. Your high-protein Korean-style meals are ready to ENJOY! 85

mealprep::honey chilli crisp chicken bow.name: Honey chilli crisp chicken Bowl
ingredients: 30-40g (1.4oz) Chilli Crisp Oil drain the extra oil (Brand: Lee Kum Kee)
steps: Trim any excess fat from the boneless chicken thighs and place them in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika, chilli crisp, and a drizzle of honey. Mix well with your hands until the colour deepens and the chicken is fully coated. Set aside to marinate.

In a large bowl, combine diced pineapple (fresh or canned), cucumber, red onion, red bell pepper, avocado, and plenty of chopped coriander. Season with salt, black pepper, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice. Mix gently until well combined. Transfer to a separate container and refrigerate to keep it fresh and crisp for the week.

Add washed uncooked basmati rice to a pot of water (use your preferred rice-towater ratio). Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 8-10 minutes or until the rice is cooked through and fluffy. Once cooked, stir in freshly chopped coriander, dried oregano, and a few cracks of chilli garlic salt. Mix gently and set aside.
Spread the marinated chicken thighs evenly on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry at 190°C / 385°F for 16-18 minutes until golden brown and juicy.

Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice into juicy cubes. Toss the pieces back onto the tray to soak up all those resting juices. Serve with the herby seasoned rice and the vibrant pineapple avocado salsa. Enjoy your flavour packed, high protein meals!

mealprep::turkish garlic beef pasta.name: Turkish Garlic Beef Pasta
ingredients: Avocado Oil Cooking Spray (or any of choice)
Add extra tomato paste if needed
steps: In a large bowl, combine a generous amount of thick yogurt (I used Skyr for its high protein content), minced garlic, dried or fresh mint, a pinch of salt, and a good squeeze of lemon juice. Stir until smooth and creamy. Transfer to a separate container and refrigerate to keep it fresh for the week.

Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add your pasta of choice (I used a mix of conchiglie and fusilli) and cook for about 8 minutes or until al dente. Reserve some of the pasta water, drain the rest, and set the pasta aside.

Heat a pan over medium heat and add avocado oil spray. Sauté chopped onions for 8–10 minutes until soft and golden brown. Then add salt, black pepper, paprika, cumin, minced garlic, and tomato paste. Cook for another 3-4 minutes until fragrant and well combined.

Increase the heat to high, add the lean ground beef to the pan, and use a spatula to break it up. Cook for about 8 minutes until browned and fully cooked through. Season again to taste with salt, pepper, and spices. Mix well until juicy and flavourful, then set aside.

In a small pan over low heat, melt a little light butter. Add paprika, chilli flakes, tomato paste, a pinch of salt, and a splash of the reserved pasta water. Stir until smooth and let it simmer gently until rich and glossy.

Divide the pasta and juicy beef into 5 equal portions. Top each with a few spoonfuls of the garlic mint yogurt and a drizzle of the chilli butter sauce. Garnish with chopped cherry tomatoes, fresh mint and chilli garlic seasoning. Your delicious high protein meals are ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::bang bang chicken rice bowls.name: Bang Bang chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1000g (35oz) Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, cubed
3 Tsp Olive Oil Calorie Bang Bang Sauce
1 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped Garlic Herb Coconut Rice
260g (9.1oz) Uncooked White Rice (freshly cooked or leftover cooked)
Extra Seasoning: 1 Tsp each Salt, Oregano, Parsley, Chilli Flakes
steps: Slice the boneless, skinless chicken thighs into bite- sized cubes. Place them in a large bowl and season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, ginger powder, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is evenly coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate.

To make the bang bang sauce, combine light mayo, yogurt, sweet chilli sauce, sriracha, rice vinegar, and freshly chopped parsley in a bowl. Mix until smooth. Separate a small amount of this sauce into another bowl—this will be used to baste the chicken during cooking.

Spread the marinated chicken cubes in a single layer on a lined sheet pan. Use a brush to lightly coat each piece with the reserved bang bang sauce. Bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 22–25 minutes until golden and slightly crispy. Once cooked, while the chicken is still hot, brush over a final layer of bang bang sauce to create a glossy, flavour-packed glaze.

In a large pan over medium heat, melt a knob of butter. Add chopped garlic and onion, and sauté for 5 minutes until soft and fragrant. Add salt, oregano, parsley, chilli flakes, and shredded coconut. Stir well and cook for another 3 minutes to toast the coconut slightly. Add your cooked rice, mix thoroughly, then finish with lemon zest and a squeeze of lemon juice. Stir until the rice is fluffy and aromatic.
Serve and divide the bang bang chicken and fragrant coconut rice evenly into 5 portions. Your high- protein meals for the week are ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::orange pepper chicken rice bow.name: Orange Pepper chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1100g (39oz) Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, trim any excess fat
steps: Trim any excess fat from the chicken thighs, then place them in a large mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, and garlic powder, then add a drizzle of honey, sriracha, fresh orange juice, orange est (add extra orange pulp for more texture when cooking), and light soy sauce. Mix thoroughly until fully combined and the colour changes. Set aside to marinate for at least 30 minutes.

Slice all the vegetables for the crunchy cucumber salad. In a large bowl or dish, add thinly sliced cucumbers, red onion, grated carrots, chopped red bell pepper, green onion/scallion, and sesame seeds, followed by a drizzle of honey and rice vinegar. Mix thoroughly until well combined, then store in separate containers and refrigerate to keep fresh and crisp for the week.

Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add the uncooked basmati rice (rinsed and drained beforehand) and cook for 8-10 minutes, until tender and fluffy. Drain any excess water and set aside. At the same time, line a sheet pan with parchment paper and spread the marinated chicken evenly in a single layer. Sprinkle a little extra garlic powder and black pepper over the top, then add additional orange juice and orange zest. This will help create a sticky, caramelized glaze as it cooks. Air fry or oven bake for 25-27 minutes at 200C / 400F, until golden brown and sizzling.

While the chicken cooks, heat a large pan over medium–low heat and melt light butter. Add plenty of chopped garlic and cook for about 4 minutes, until fragrant and infused into the butter. Add the leftover cooked rice, mix well, and allow it to heat through. Let the rice cool slightly before adding freshly chopped parsley, a pinch of salt, and chilli flakes. Mix until fluffy.

Once the chicken is cooked, slice it into juicy cubes, mixing it with the resting juices in the sheet pan for extra flavour. Serve and portion with the fluffy garlic rice and crunchy cucumber salad, then ENJOY!

mealprep::middle eastern honey garlic ch.name: Middle eastern Honey Garlic chicken Bowls
ingredients: 1 Tsp Turmeric Salt Paprika Fresh Parsley, chopped
steps: Trim any excess fat from the boneless chicken thighs, then slice into cubes and place in a mixing bowl. Season with salt, black pepper, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cinnamon, and coriander powder, then add a drizzle of olive oil, yogurt, and honey. Mix until fully coated and the colour deepens, then set aside to marinate.
In a large mixing bowl, add sliced red onion, plenty of freshly chopped parsley, sumac, a pinch of salt, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Mix well, then store in a separate container and refrigerate.

Rinse the uncooked basmati rice, then place it in a pot of water and bring to a boil. Reduce to a medium low simmer and cook until fully tender and fluffy. Meanwhile, line a sheet pan with parchment paper and spread the marinated chicken in a single, even layer. Oven bake or air fry for 25 minutes, until golden orange with crispy edges.

In a large pan over low heat, melt butter, then add a few cinnamon sticks. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until fragrant, before adding the cooked basmati rice, along with turmeric, salt, paprika, and freshly chopped parsley. Mix until every grain is golden yellow, fluffy, and aromatic.
Serve the crispy, juicy honey garlic chicken with the pickled red onions, then ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy honey butter steak pota.name: creamy Honey Butter Steak & Potatoes
ingredients: 1000g (35.2oz) Raw Sirloin or Top Sirloin Steaks, cut into cubes
2 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, finely chopped Tuscan Potatoes
3 Tsp Olive Oil or Cooking Spray
120g (4.2oz) Sun-dried Tomatoes, oil drained and chopped
steps: Slice the sirloin steaks into 1-inch cubes and place them in a mixing bowl. Season generously with salt, black pepper, parsley, chilli flakes, paprika, and garlic powder, then drizzle with olive oil. Mix well until fully coated and the colour deepens.

Slice the potatoes into small cubes. Place them in a large tray and season with salt, paprika, mixed herbs, and garlic powder, followed by a drizzle of olive oil. Mix until well coated, then spread them in an even layer on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry for 20 minutes at 200°C / 400°F, until golden yellow and crispy.

Heat a pan over medium heat and melt butter. Cook the steak bites for 3-4 minutes per side, until perfectly seared. Lower the heat, then add extra butter, a drizzle of honey, and fresh parsley. Mix until all steak pieces are coated in this rich honey butter glaze, then set aside. Repeat with the remaining steak bites.

In the same pan over low heat, add the chopped sun-dried tomatoes, along with salt and mixed herbs. Cook for about 2 minutes, then add evaporated milk, light cream cheese, Dijon mustard, and freshly chopped parsley. Stir continuously until the sauce becomes smooth, rich, and creamy.
Keeping the heat on low, add the crispy potatoes to the pan. Fold gently to coat the potatoes evenly in the creamy sauce. Serve and portion with the honey butter steak bites, then ENJOY!

Avocado Oil Cooking Spray Mashed Potatoes
1000g (35.2oz) Raw Potatoes, peeled cut into chunks
3-4 Sprigs of Fresh Thyme or Dried Thyme
250g (8.8oz) Light Evaporated Milk Garlic Parm Broccoli
steps: Slice the steaks into 1-inch cubes and place them in a salt, and chilli flakes, then spray with avocado cooking mixing bowl. Season generously with salt, black pepper, oil. Mix well with your hands until fully coated and spread Italian herbs, garlic powder, parsley, and a drizzle into an even layer. Oven roast or air fry for 18 minutes at of olive oil. Mix until evenly coated, then set aside to 180°C / 370°F, until golden and crispy. marinate briefly.

mealprep::crispy tandoori chicken chips.name: crispy Tandoori chicken & chips
ingredients: 1100g (39oz) Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, trim excess fat
1100g (39oz) Uncooked White or Yellow Potatoes, cut into fries

1 Tsp each Salt, Black Pepper, Paprika, Cumin

mealprep::honey buffalo chicken rice bow.name: Honey Buffalo chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: ½ Tsp each: Garlic Powder, Smoked Paprika, Black Pepper With
200g Uncooked Basmati Rice/Approx 135g Cooked Rice per serving
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes and place them in a bowl. Add minced garlic, black pepper, onion powder, paprika, salt, and olive oil. Toss until evenly coated, then set aside to marinate.

Prepare the honey buffalo sauce by blending mayo, buffalo hot sauce, honey, lemon juice, garlic powder, paprika, and black pepper until smooth and creamy. Transfer to a bottle for easy use throughout the week. (Tip: Start cooking the white rice now so it’s ready by the time everything else is done)

For the crunchy slaw, combine thinly sliced red cabbage, grated carrots, chopped green onion, and fresh cilantro in a bowl. Add mayo, honey, salt, and black pepper, then toss until well incorporated. Store separately for easier reheating.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat and melt butter. Add the marinated chicken and cook for 4 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Lower the heat, then drizzle in honey and buffalo sauce, stirring until the chicken is well coated and the sauce thickens slightly. Set aside and repeat for any remaining chicken.
Assemble the meal by serving the hot rice with crispy honey buffalo chicken and crunchy slaw on the side. Drizzle with extra sauce, garnish with fresh cilantro, and ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy tuscan chicken.name: creamy Tuscan chicken
ingredients: 210g Uncooked Basmati/Approx 140g Cooked Rice per serving
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes and place them in a bowl. Add minced garlic, dried basil, chili flakes, salt, smoked paprika, and a drizzle of olive oil. Toss until well coated, then set aside to marinate.

For the creamy sauce, blend milk, cream cheese, grated Parmesan, tomato paste, dried basil, and salt to taste until completely smooth. Set aside. (Tip: Start cooking the rice now so it’s ready when serving the creamy chicken)

Heat a pan over medium-high heat and add a little olive oil or butter. Cook the chicken for 4 minutes on one side, then flip. Lower the heat and pour in the creamy sauce, followed by cherry tomatoes and spinach. Stir lightly, cover with a lid, and let it simmer on low heat for 5–7 minutes.

Remove the lid halfway through and stir gently to prevent sticking while allowing the sauce to thicken. By now, the chicken will be coated in the creamy sauce, the spinach will have wilted, and the cherry tomatoes will be soft and bursting with flavor.
Serve the creamy chicken over hot herb-seasoned rice, distribute evenly, and ENJOY!

mealprep::korean beef rice bowls.name: Korean Beef Rice Bowls
ingredients: 20g (1oz) Reduced Fat Butter (for cooking) Lemon Mayo
210g (7.4oz) Uncooked Short Grain or Basmati Rice/Approx 140g Cooked Rice per serving
steps: In a bowl, combine dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, gochujang paste, and sesame seeds. Whisk until well mixed and set aside. (Tip: Start cooking the rice now so it’s ready when serving)

Heat a pan over high heat and melt butter. Add the ground beef and cook for 6–8 minutes, using a spatula to break it up as it browns. Once fully cooked, pour in the prepared sauce along with chopped green onion. Stir and cook for another 5 minutes until well combined, golden brown, and slightly crispy. Remove from heat.

For the quick pickled cucumber slaw, combine sliced cucumber, grated carrot, soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic powder, chili flakes, and sesame seeds in a large bowl. Mix well with a spoon, then store separately to keep the texture fresh.
To make the spicy mayo, whisk together mayo, sriracha, garlic powder, and lemon juice until smooth. Transfer to a squeeze bottle for easy use throughout the week.

Assemble the meal by serving the flavorful beef with the hot rice, adding a side of pickled cucumber slaw and a generous drizzle of spicy sauce. Garnish with extra green onion and sesame seeds, then ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy butter chicken pasta.name: creamy Butter chicken Pasta
ingredients: 270g Uncooked Penne Pasta (Or use protein pasta)
Spice Mix: ½ Tsp each Smoked Paprika, Turmeric, Garam Masala, Chilli Powder, Salt, Black Pepper
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes and place them in a bowl. Add minced garlic, smoked paprika, turmeric, garam masala, chili powder, salt, black pepper, yogurt, and lemon juice. Mix until evenly coated, then set aside to marinate.

Heat a pan over medium heat and melt butter. Add thinly sliced onion and cook for 5 minutes until soft and fragrant. Stir in the spice mix, ensuring the onions are well coated, then add canned chopped tomatoes and cashew nuts.

Mix well and let everything cook for another 5 minutes on medium-low heat. Turn off the heat and stir in yogurt, then let the mixture cool for 5 minutes. (Tip: Transfer to a large dish for faster cooling)
While the sauce cools, bring a pot of water to a boil and cook the penne pasta for 7–8 minutes until al dente. Once cooked, reserve 1 cup of pasta water, then drain the rest.
Transfer the cooled sauce to a blender, add ½ cup of pasta water, and blend for 2–3 minutes until completely smooth and creamy.

Reheat the pan over medium-high heat, melt butter, and cook the marinated chicken for 4 minutes per side until golden brown. Lower the heat, then pour in the butter chicken sauce and add the cooked pasta. Stir until well combined and heated through.
Garnish with fresh coriander and white sauce, then evenly portion, serve and ENJOY!

mealprep::crispy potatoes creamy eggs.name: crispy Potatoes & creamy eggs
steps: Cut the potatoes into small cubes and season with mixed with the vegetables and turkey bacon. Cook oregano, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. for another 5 minutes until golden brown and crispy, Toss until evenly coated, then spread them on a then set aside. plate and microwave for 5 minutes until soft and
fork-tender. In the same pan over low heat, pour in the whisked

eggs. Stir gently with a spatula for 4–5 minutes, Heat a pan over medium heat and add olive oil. scrambling them slowly. Just before they fully cook, Cook the microwaved potatoes for 4 minutes to add cream cheese and fold it in gently until melted, crisp them up slightly, then add paprika, chopped creating a fluffy, creamy texture. red onion, red bell pepper, and chopped turkey
bacon. Serve the creamy scrambled eggs alongside the
crispy potato mix, drizzle with spicy mayo, garnish Stir everything together until the potatoes are well with fresh parsley, and ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy garlic beef pasta.name: creamy Garlic Beef Pasta
ingredients: 250g Uncooked Fusilli Pasta (or pasta of choice)
steps: Chop the garlic cloves, red bell pepper, and tomatoes. Heat a pan over medium heat, add olive oil, and sauté the garlic for 2 minutes until fragrant. Then, add the chopped bell peppers and tomatoes.
Season with Italian herbs, paprika, salt, and black pepper, then cook for 5 minutes until soft and fragrant. Transfer the mixture to a dish and let it cool while preparing the next step.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add the fusilli pasta and cook for 8 minutes until al dente. Reserve ½ cup of pasta water, then drain the rest and set the pasta aside.
In a blender cup, combine the cooled vegetable mixture, cream cheese, and reserved pasta water. Blend for 2 minutes until completely smooth and creamy. The sauce should be a vibrant orange color.

Return the pan to high heat, add a drizzle of olive oil, and cook the ground beef with extra seasoning (Italian herbs, paprika, salt, black pepper) for 6–8 minutes. Use a spatula to break up the meat while cooking until fully browned with no pink bits remaining.

Lower the heat and pour the creamy sauce over the beef, followed by the cooked pasta. Stir gently until well combined and indulgent. Let it simmer on low heat for 3–4 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Garnish with fresh parsley and parmesan, then serve and distribute evenly. ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken fajita rice.name: chicken Fajita Rice
ingredients: 230g (8.1oz) Uncooked Basmati Rice/ Approx. 520g Cooked Rice
1 Tsp each: Salt, Paprika, Cumin, Garlic Powder, Turmeric
75g (2.7oz) Green Bell Pepper, thinly sliced Sauce
steps: Butterfly slice the chicken breasts into thinner fillets/cutlets. Season both sides evenly with oregano, garlic powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and olive oil. Rub the spices into the chicken until fully coated, then set aside.

Thinly slice the onions and bell peppers for later. For the creamy green sauce, blend yogurt, mayo, garlic cloves, fresh coriander, salt, black pepper, and lemon juice until smooth. Adjust seasoning to taste and set aside.
Heat a pan over medium heat and add olive oil. Cook the chicken cutlets for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and fully cooked. Set aside and repeat until all the chicken is done.

In the same pan over medium heat, add the sliced onions and bell peppers. Sauté for 4–5 minutes until soft and fragrant. Stir in the washed uncooked rice along with the spice mix (salt, paprika, cumin, garlic powder, turmeric).
Mix everything well and toast the rice for 2 minutes, then add water or stock. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes until the rice is fluffy and fully cooked.
Slice the chicken cutlets into thin strips, then serve over the fajita-seasoned rice. Drizzle generously with the creamy green sauce and ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy spicy chipotle chicken.name: creamy Spicy chipotle chicken Pasta
ingredients: 1.5 tsp each: Salt, Cumin, Smoked Paprika
200g (7oz) Uncooked Penne Pasta (or pasta of choice)
1 Tsp Chipotle Chilli Flakes (omit for less heat)
steps: Start by slicing the chicken breasts into cubes, then season with chipotle paste, minced garlic, honey, lime juice, salt, cumin and smoked paprika. Mix till the color changes then set aside to marinate
Now cook your pasta. Get water in a pot, bring to a boil then add your pasta and boil for 8-9 mins till al dente. Once cooked, remove ½ cup pasta water and drain the rest then set aside
Next for the creamy sauce, in a blender cup add milk, cream cheese, parmesan,chipotle paste, chili flakes, salt, pasta water. Blend till smooth and creamy!
Then get a pan on medium heat, melt down butter and cook the chicken for 8-10 min (4-5 mins each side) till golden brown and charred

Once the chicken is cooked, lower the heat then add the cooked pasta with the creamy sauce over it and give it all a thorough gentle mix till well incorporated. Let it simmer for 1-2 mins to thicken the sauce then you’re ready to serve and distribute evenly, garnish with fresh parsley and ENJOY!

mealprep::garlic herb chicken rice bowls.name: Garlic Herb chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 20g (1.5 Tbsp) Garlic Paste or Minced Garlic
1 Tsp Chilli Flakes (omit for milder flavour)
steps: Place the chicken thighs in a mixing bowl. Add garlic paste, salt, black pepper, mixed herbs, parsley, paprika, chili flakes, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until well coated, then set aside to marinate.

While the chicken marinates, cook the rice by boiling it in a pot of water for 8–10 minutes. Once done, drain any excess water and set aside. While the rice cooks, chop the garlic, red onion, and bell peppers.
Heat a pan over medium heat, melt butter, and cook the chicken thighs for 5–6 minutes per side until a deep golden crust forms. Once cooked, set aside to rest. Repeat for the rest of the chicken.

In the same pan over medium-low heat, sauté the chopped garlic, onion, and bell peppers for 5–6 minutes until soft and fragrant. Stir in extra seasoning (salt, pepper, mixed herbs, parsley, paprika) till the the onions and peppers catch colour
Add the cooked basmati rice and mix well until every grain is coated in the spices and takes on a vibrant orange color.
Garnish the rice with freshly chopped parsley. Slice the chicken thighs into cubes, serve with the rice, and ENJOY!

mealprep::teriyaki beef fried rice.name: Teriyaki Beef Fried Rice
ingredients: 20g (1.5 Tbsp) Minced Garlic or Garlic Paste
Dash of Milk for desired consistency Fried Rice
200g Dry Weight / ~520g Cooked White Rice (I use basmati but you can use short grain rice)
steps: First chop up all your vegetables, this includes green onions, white onion, garlic and coriander/cilantro. And also cook your rice by boiling in a pot or use leftover rice
Get a pan on high heat, melt down some butter then cook all your ground beef for 6-8 mins till completely brown and no pink bits remain. Use a spatula to break up the meat to cook quicker
Once cooked lower the heat to medium heat, add lots of minced garlic, soy sauce, honey or maple syrup, rice vinegar, green part of the green onion and sesame seeds
Mix till well incorporated then cook the beef for another 5 mins till golden brown and slightly crispy. Once cooked set the beef aside
Now in the same pan add chopped garlic white onion and the white part of green onion. Cook these for 5-6 mins on medium heat till soft and fragrant then push to the side of the pan
Add in your whisked eggs and use a spatula to keep stirring gently till the eggs are scrambled. Once ready, mix with all the veg
Now make a well in the middle and add in your cooked rice with more soy sauce, chilli garlic sauce, green onion, coriander and sesame seeds.
Mix everything till well combined and every grain of rice is coloured
For the Yum Yum sauce, In a bowl combine yogurt, light mayo, ketchup, sriracha, garlic powder and a dash of milk. Mix till smooth then bottle this up!
Finally serve with the beef and the fried rice then ENJOY your high protein meals for the week!

mealprep::garlic parmesan steak alfredo.name: Garlic Parmesan Steak Alfredo Pasta
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Topside/ Top Sirloin, 1 inch cubed
2 Tsp Olive Oil Cooking & Final Coating
30g (2 Tbsp) Garlic Paste or Minced Garlic
1 Tsp each: Salt, Black Pepper, Italian Herbs, Parsley, Smoked Paprika
210g (7.4oz) Uncooked Linguine Pasta (or pasta of choice)
50g ( 1/4 Cup) Pasta Water (Optional for sauce consistency)
steps: Slice the beef steaks into bite-sized cubes. Season steak bites are well coated in this flavourful butter, with salt, black pepper, Italian herbs, parsley, then set aside. Repeat for the remaining steak bites. garlic powder, smoked paprika, and olive oil. Mix
well until evenly coated. In the same pan over low heat, sauté chopped

garlic and onion for 5 minutes until soft and Bring a pot of water to a boil, then add your pasta fragrant. Stir in the extra seasonings (salt, pepper, of choice. Cook for 8–10 minutes until al dente. Italian herbs, parsley, smoked paprika). Reserve 1/4 cup pasta water, then drain the rest and
set aside. Mix until the spices are well combined, then add

evaporated milk, cream cheese, parmesan, and Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add butter, and reserved pasta water. Stir gently until smooth and sear the steak bites for 2 minutes per side until a thickened. golden-brown crust forms.

Add the cooked pasta into the creamy sauce, Once cooked, lower the heat and add extra butter, stirring until fully combined and coated. Serve with minced garlic, and grated parmesan. Stir until the the juicy steak bites, and your high-protein meal prep is ready for the week!

mealprep::honey bbq chicken mac cheese.name: Honey BBQ chicken Mac & cheese
ingredients: 60g (2.1oz) Buffalo Hot Sauce (or any hot sauce)
steps: Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized cubes. In a bowl, mix with minced garlic, black pepper, paprika, salt, and olive oil until evenly coated. Adjust seasoning if you need a deeper color.
Meanwhile, cook the macaroni pasta in a pot of water for 8 mins until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and lightly coat with cooking spray. Sear the chicken cubes for 3–4 minutes per side until they are golden brown and nicely charred.
Turn off the heat completely, then drizzle honey and BBQ sauce over the chicken. Toss well until every piece is coated and juicy, then transfer to a bowl. Repeat for the remaining chicken.
In the same pan over low heat, add milk, hot sauce (such as buffalo sauce), cream cheese, and shredded cheddar cheese. Whisk gently until the mixture is smooth and creamy.
Stir the cooked macaroni into the cheese sauce until thoroughly combined. Garnish with chopped parsley, then serve with the delicious BBQ chicken, divide into 4 equal servings and ENJOY!

mealprep::garlic parm chicken creamy mas.name: Garlic Parm chicken & creamy Mashed Potatoes
ingredients: ½ tsp each: Garlic Powder, Italian Herbs, Salt, Paprika
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into thinner halves. In a bowl, season with garlic powder, Italian herbs, salt, paprika, olive oil, and grated parmesan. Rub in thoroughly to ensure both sides are evenly coated, then set aside to marinate.
Cut the washed potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Place them in a pot of water and boil for 12–14 minutes until fork-tender. Drain the water and allow the remaining steam to evaporate any excess moisture.
Using a potato masher, mash the potatoes until smooth and fluffy. Be careful not to over-mash; you want to maintain a pleasant texture.
Stir in a knob of butter, evaporated milk, garlic powder, salt, and freshly cracked black pepper. Mix until the mash is smooth and well combined.

Heat a pan over medium-low heat and cook the marinated chicken for 4 minutes per side until golden and crispy, forming a flavor-packed crust. Once cooked, set the chicken aside. Repeat for the remaining chicken cutlets.
In the same pan on low heat, add the same spices used for the chicken to toast in the remaining oil. Then, stir in milk, light cream cheese, and parmesan until the sauce is smooth and well blended.

Continue stirring the sauce until it becomes completely smooth and creamy, letting it bubble slightly to thicken. Chop the rested chicken into bite-sized pieces, then serve by layering the creamy mash with the sauce and topping with the chopped chicken. ENJOY!

mealprep::chilli peanut chicken noodles.name: chilli Peanut chicken Noodles
ingredients: Big Handful Fresh Coriander/ Cilantro, chopped
1 Tsp Chilli Flakes (omit for less heat)
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes. In a bowl, marinate with black pepper, garlic & ginger paste, chilli flakes, onion powder, sriracha, and soy sauce. Mix until the color deepens, then set aside.
While the chicken marinates, cook the noodles for 5–6 minutes (or follow package instructions). Reserve some noodle water, then drain and rinse the noodles under cold water.

Heat a pan over medium heat and add butter . Cook the chicken cubes for 3–4 minutes per side until golden and crispy. Once cooked, add extra butter and chopped coriander, tossing until well coated. Set aside.

In the same pan over low heat, combine peanut butter, soy sauce, sriracha, rice vinegar, optional chilli flakes, and a splash of reserved noodle water. Stir continuously for 3–4 minutes until the sauce is silky smooth.
Toss the cooked noodles in the sauce along with chopped green onions, additional coriander, and sesame seeds. Mix thoroughly, then serve alongside the juicy chicken and ENJOY!

mealprep::korean bbq chicken rice bowls.name: Korean BBQ chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1 Tbsp Sesame Seeds & Tangy Cucumber Salad
1 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes or Chilli Flakes (Omit for less heat)
200g (7oz) Uncooked Basmati Rice (≈130g or 4.6oz cooked rice per serving)
steps: Trim any excess fat from your boneless chicken thighs. In a mixing bowl, marinate the chicken with black pepper, garlic & ginger paste, gochujang, sriracha, sesame seeds, and light soy sauce. Mix thoroughly then set aside to marinate.
While the chicken marinates, cook your rice by boiling it or use leftover rice as a time-saving option.

Thinly slice the cucumbers and place them in a bowl. Add salt, minced garlic, gochujang, honey, rice vinegar, sesame seeds, red pepper flakes (optional), and soy sauce. Toss well until evenly combined.
Transfer the cucumber salad to a separate container and refrigerate—it will keep fresh for the week.
Spread the marinated chicken thighs evenly on a lined sheet pan (use baking paper). Oven bake or air fry at 200°C/400°F for 16–18 minutes until the chicken has a beautifully golden char and juicy.
Let the chicken thighs rest for 3–4 minutes, then slice them into bite-sized cubes. Serve with the rice and cucumber salad, and ENJOY!

mealprep::butter chicken crispy masala p.name: Butter chicken & crispy Masala Potatoes
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, cubed
20g (1.5 Tbsp) Minced Garlic or Garlic Paste
½ Tsp each: Salt, Garam Masala, Paprika, Turmeric
steps: Trim any excess fat from the chicken thighs and cut into cubes. In a bowl, marinate the chicken with minced garlic, salt, garam masala, paprika, turmeric, chilli powder, yogurt, and lemon juice.
Mix the chicken thoroughly until its color deepens, then set aside. Next, slice the potatoes into small cubes and season with salt, paprika, garam masala, garlic powder, and olive oil.
Mix the potatoes till well coated then place on a sheet pan/tray and oven or air fry for 20-23mins at 200C/400F till golden and crispy

Spread the marinated chicken on a separate sheet pan or tray and cook in the oven or air fryer at 200°C/400°F for 18–20 minutes until golden and juicy. (Tip: You can cook the chicken and potatoes simultaneously if your equipment allows)
In a pan over medium heat, melt butter and add sliced onions, salt, garam masala, paprika, and turmeric. Sauté for 5–6 minutes until the onions are soft and fragrant.
Make a well in the center of the pan and add minced garlic, cashew nuts, and the tomatoes (canned or fresh). Stir thoroughly, then cover with a lid and let it simmer on low heat for 6–8 minutes.
Stir the mixture, then allow it to cool before transferring to a blender. Blend for 2–3 minutes until completely smooth.
Return the blended sauce to the pan and add yogurt, evaporated milk, and a few small cubes of butter. Stir gently until the sauce becomes creamy.

Fold in the cooked chicken, mixing thoroughly so each piece is well coated. Finally, distribute the butter chicken with the masala potatoes into 4 portions and drizzle with the white sauce then ENJOY!

mealprep::nacho cheese beef loaded fries.name: Nacho cheese Beef Loaded Fries
ingredients: 1 Tsp each: Salt, Black Pepper, Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Paprika, Oregano, Cumin
½ or 1 Tsp Chilli Powder Jalapeno Beef
900g Raw Potatoes, cut into thin fries
steps: Slice the washed potatoes into thin French fries. Mix with 1 serving of taco spice mix and a drizzle of olive oil until evenly coated.
Arrange the fries on a lined sheet pan and oven bake or air fry at 200°C/400°F for 20–25 minutes until golden and crispy.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add butter, and cook all the ground beef for 5–7 minutes, breaking it up with a spatula until browned

Once the beef is browned, stir in 1 serving of taco spice mix, tomato paste, chopped jalapeños, and jalapeño juice. Cook for 2 more minutes until the mixture is juicy and flavours intensify then set aside

Using the same pan on low heat, add evaporated milk, American cheese slices, sharp cheddar, and a splash of jalapeño juice. Stir until the cheeses melt and the sauce becomes silky smooth. (This sauce can be stored in a container and refrigerated for later use)

Prepare the side salad by combining chopped tomatoes, red onion, red and green bell peppers, avocado, coriander, lime juice, honey, salt, and black pepper in a bowl. Toss until well combined and store separately.
To serve, distribute the crispy taco fries evenly and top with the juicy jalapeño beef. Drizzle generously with the nacho cheese sauce and serve alongside the fresh salad. ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy garlic herb cajun chick.name: creamy Garlic Herb cajun chicken
ingredients: 800g Raw White Potatoes peeled and diced into 1 inch chuncks »»
¾ Tsp Each Salt, Italian Herbs, Chilli Flakes, Garlic, Onion Powder, Parsley, Paprika
steps: First get your boneless skinless chicken thighs, season with salt, Italian herbs, chilli flakes, parsley, garlic and onion powder, paprika and olive oil. Mix till well coated and combined
Next peel all your potatoes then slice into chunks. Place them in a pot of cold water then boil for 12-14 mins until fork tender. Drain all the water then cover with a lid for 5 mins to steam
Uncover then mash the potatoes using a masher or ricer till smooth and fluffy. Note - don’t overly mash them!
Next add a knob of room temperature butter with warm milk, salt and garlic powder. Use a spatula to mix everything till smooth and combined
Now place all your chicken thighs flat and evenly spread on a lined sheet pan. Now oven bake or air fry for 22-25mins at 200C / 400F. The chicken should come out golden and charred!
For the cream sauce get a pan on low heat, melt down some butter then add the spice mix consisting of the same spices used for the chicken. Toast the spices for 2 mins in the butter
Add milk followed by cream cheese, parmesan cheese and chopped spinach. Mix gently till everything is melted, thickened and the spinach wilted
Finally when the sauce is ready, chop your chicken into delicious cubes then serve with the mashed potatoes topped with the sauce and ENJOY!

mealprep::halal cart chicken rice bowls.name: Halal cart chicken Rice Bowls
ingredients: (Optional) Drizzle of Sriracha when serving Salad
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into small ½-inch cubes. In a bowl, combine with minced garlic, salt, oregano, smoked paprika, chilli flakes, cumin, olive oil, and lemon juice. Mix thoroughly until well coated.
Prepare the white sauce by whisking together yogurt, mayo, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and a squeeze of lemon juice until smooth. Transfer to a bottle and refrigerate.
Cook the white rice according to package instructions. Meanwhile, chop the lettuce, tomatoes, and cucumbers for the salad. Season the salad to taste - I like a light sprinkle of chilli flakes.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat with a knob of butter. Cook the marinated chicken for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and juicy.
Reduce the heat to low and add extra butter and freshly chopped coriander to the chicken. Stir until the chicken is evenly coated, then remove from heat and set aside. Repeat for all the chicken.
In the same pan, add chopped garlic and either harissa or tomato paste. Sauté for 2 minutes, then add additional spices with a dash of water. Stir for another minute until well combined.

Add the cooked rice to the pan and mix thoroughly until every grain is infused with the vibrant spice mix. Finally, serve the red rice alongside the chicken and salad, drizzle with the white sauce, and ENJOY!

mealprep::buffalo chicken potato bowls.name: Buffalo chicken Potato Bowls
ingredients: 2 Tsp Olive Oil Cooking & Final Glaze
½ Tsp each: Salt, Black Pepper, Garlic Powder, Cumin
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into small ½-inch cubes. Season with salt, parsley, garlic powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and olive oil. Toss until evenly coated, then set aside.

Chop the potatoes into small cubes and spread them on a lined sheet pan (use baking paper). Season with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, cumin, and parsley, then drizzle with olive oil or lightly coat with cooking spray. Mix till well coated.
Air fry or bake the seasoned potatoes in a preheated oven at 200°C/400°F for 18–20 minutes until golden and crispy.
While the potatoes cook, prepare the creamy buffalo sauce. In a bowl, combine buffalo hot sauce, yogurt, mayo, and honey. Mix until the sauce is smooth and slightly thick.
For the fresh salad, combine chopped tomatoes, red onions, cucumber, green onions, and parsley in a large bowl. Season with your preferred spices and a squeeze of lemon juice; toss thoroughly .

Heat a pan over medium-high heat and add butter. Cook the marinated chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and charred. Once cooked, lower the heat and add additional buffalo hot sauce and a bit more butter, stirring to coat the chicken evenly.
Ensure the chicken is well coated, then serve it alongside the crispy potatoes and fresh salad. Drizzle with the creamy buffalo sauce, and ENJOY!

mealprep::chimichurri garlic steak bites.name: chimichurri Garlic Steak Bites & cheesy Mashed Potatoes
ingredients: Flaky Salt to Garnish Mashed Potatoes
steps: Slice the beef steaks into cubes. In a bowl, season with salt, black pepper, parsley, oregano, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and olive oil. Mix until evenly coated.

Peel the potatoes and cut them into thick chunks. Place them in a pot of water and boil for 12–14 minutes until fork-tender. Drain then cover with a lid for 5 minutes to allow the remaining steam to evaporate any excess moisture.
Using a potato masher, mash the potatoes until smooth. Stir in evaporated milk, shredded mozzarella, garlic powder, salt, and pepper until the mash is creamy and cheesy.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and add a little light butter. Sear the steak bites for about 2 minutes per side until they develop a golden, charred crust.

Lower the heat to low, then add extra butter, minced garlic, plenty of parsley, chilli flakes, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Stir until the beef is thoroughly coated in this chimichurri flavoured butter.
Serve the juicy steak bites alongside the cheesy mashed potatoes. Divide evenly into 4 containers for your high-protein meal prep, and ENJOY!

mealprep::butter chicken loaded fries.name: Butter chicken Loaded Fries
ingredients: ½ Tsp Chilli Powder Chicken Sauce
steps: Slice the chicken into cubes and place in a bowl. Add fresh garlic, butter chicken spice mix, yogurt, and lemon juice. Toss until evenly coated and set aside to marinate.

Wash the potatoes and cut them into fries. Season with salt, garlic, paprika, cumin, garam masala, and olive oil. Toss well, then spread on a baking tray. Air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20 minutes until golden and crispy.
While the fries are cooking, heat a pan over medium- high heat and melt butter. Add sliced onions and sauté for 5 minutes until soft and fragrant.
Stir in chopped tomatoes, extra butter chicken spice mix, and cashew nuts. Simmer for 8–10 minutes until the mixture reduces to a sauce-like consistency.
Turn off the heat and stir in yogurt. Let the mixture cool slightly before transferring it to a blender. Blend for 2–3 minutes until smooth. Allow the sauce to cool further if needed.
In a clean pan over high heat, cook the marinated chicken for 8 minutes until golden. Reduce the heat to medium and add the blended butter chicken sauce. Stir until the chicken is well coated.

Gently combine the chicken with the crispy fries. Top with cheese and cover the pan for 2 minutes to allow the cheese to melt. Garnish with freshly chopped coriander and a sprinkle of chilli flakes. Serve and ENJOY!

mealprep::beef shawarma mac n cheese.name: Beef Shawarma Mac n cheese
ingredients: 20g (1oz) Reduced Fat Butter (for cooking) n Cheese
steps: Slice your sirloin beef into extra thin strips. Place in Mix well for 1-2 minutes until evenly coated and a bowl and add 2 servings of shawarma spice mix, golden brown. Remove the beef from the pan and olive oil, and lemon juice. Mix in thoroughly and set set aside. Repeat for the remaining beef. aside to marinate.

In the same pan, add extra shawarma spice mix Bring a pot of water to a boil and add your to the leftover oil and toast for 2 minutes. Stir in macaroni pasta. Cook for 8 minutes until al dente, evaporated milk, cream cheese, cheddar, and then reserve some pasta water, drain the rest, and some reserved pasta water. set the pasta aside.

Keep the heat at medium-low and stir the sauce Prepare the red onion mix by thinly slicing 2 large until it’s smooth and creamy, letting it bubble red onions and combining them in a bowl with slightly. Then add the cooked pasta and toss until chopped parsley, sumac, and lemon juice. Mix well creamy and well combined. and set aside.

Divide the mac n cheese with the shawarma beef Heat a pan on high heat with some butter. Sear the and sumac onion mix evenly into 4 containers, marinated beef for 1–2 minutes, then reduce the heat serve, and ENJOY! to medium and add extra butter and parsley.

mealprep::peri peri chicken spicy rice.name: Peri Peri chicken & Spicy Rice
ingredients: 1 Tsp each: Salt, Cumin, Smoked Paprika, Chilli Powder, Turmeric
steps: Trim any excess fat from the chicken thighs. In a bowl, combine with peri-peri spice mix, Nando’s marinade, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Toss until well coated, then let it marinate for at least 30 minutes—or overnight for best flavor.
Prepare the peri-naise by mixing mayo, Nando’s hot sauce, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and a squeeze of lemon juice until completely smooth. Transfer to a bottle and store in the fridge.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat and add a little butter. Cook the marinated chicken for about 5 minutes on each side until golden and juicy. Remove from the pan and set aside. Repeat for the remaining chicken.
In the same pan, add chopped garlic, onion, and bell pepper. Sauté for 3–4 minutes until soft, then stir in salt, cumin, smoked paprika, chilli powder, and turmeric.
Mix in the tomato paste and cook for another 3–4 minutes. Then add the cooked white rice and fresh parsley, stirring well until every grain of rice turns a bright yellow-orange color.
Serve and distribute the spicy rice evenly with the chicken and a generous drizzle of peri-naise then ENJOY!

mealprep::black pepper beef garlic fried.name: Black Pepper Beef & Garlic Fried Rice
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, thinly sliced
200g (7oz) Uncooked White Rice/540g Cooked Leftover Rice (basmati or jasmine)
1 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes to garnish
steps: Slice the beef steaks into extra thin strips. In a bowl, combine with dark and light soy sauce, black pepper, garlic powder, baking soda, cornflour, and olive oil. Toss the beef until velvety, then set aside to marinate.
Prepare the black pepper sauce by whisking together beef broth, light and dark soy sauce, hoisin sauce, rice vinegar, black pepper, and cornflour in a separate bowl until smooth.

Heat a pan over high heat and add a little butter. Cook the marinated beef strips for 1–2 minutes per side until a golden, crispy crust forms. Remove the beef from the pan and set aside. Repeat for the remaining beef.
In the same pan, add sliced onions and bell peppers. Sauté for about 5 minutes until soft and fragrant, then return the crispy beef to the pan and pour in the black pepper sauce.
Stir the mixture and let it come to a gentle bubble so the sauce thickens slightly. Simmer on low heat for 3–5 minutes until everything is heated through, then remove from the heat and set aside.
For the garlic fried rice, heat another pan (or reuse the cleaned pan) over medium heat and melt a bit of butter. Sauté chopped garlic until golden and slightly crispy.
Add the leftover cooked rice along with a dash of soy sauce and red pepper flakes. Toss thoroughly until well combined. Serve the garlic rice alongside the black pepper beef and ENJOY!

mealprep::crispy spicy beef noodles.name: crispy Spicy Beef Noodles
steps: Prepare the sauce by combining light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, honey, sweet chilli sauce, rice vinegar, chilli garlic paste, red pepper flakes, sesame seeds, and water in a bowl. Stir until well blended.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat and melt a little butter. Add all the ground beef and cook for 6–8 minutes until browned and most of the liquid evaporates, breaking it up with a spatula while it cooks.
