Reduce the heat to medium and stir in half of the prepared sauce along with chopped green onions. Continue cooking for 5 minutes until the beef becomes golden and crispy, then transfer to a plate.
Cook the noodles by placing them in boiling water for 5 minutes. Drain immediately to prevent overcooking.

In the same pan, add chopped garlic, ginger, and green onions. Sauté for 3–4 minutes until fragrant, then add sliced red onions and bell peppers. Cook for an additional 5 minutes until the vegetables are soft.
Return the crispy beef to the pan and add the remaining sauce along with the cooked noodles and extra green onions. Toss gently until everything is evenly coated and heated through. Serve and ENJOY!

mealprep::cheesy beef bolognaise potato.name: cheesy Beef Bolognaise & Potato Skillet
steps: Wash the potatoes and cut them into small cubes. Finely chop the onions, celery, and garlic, and grate 2 medium carrots.
Place the potato cubes on a lined baking tray. Season with garlic powder, chilli flakes, paprika, oregano, basil, and a drizzle of olive oil. Toss until well coated.

Air fry or oven-bake the potatoes at 200°C (400°F) for 20 minutes. Meanwhile, heat a pan over medium heat, add light butter, and sauté the chopped onions, celery, garlic, and grated carrots for 5–6 minutes until soft.
Season the vegetable mix with salt and pepper, cook for another 2 minutes, then remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add a little extra light butter and increase the heat to medium-high. Cook the ground beef for 5–7 minutes, breaking it up with a spatula until slightly charred and fully cooked.
Stir in tomato paste, salt, basil, oregano, chilli flakes, and paprika, then add ½ cans of chopped tomatoes. Let the sauce simmer on low for 5 minutes, then stir in the reserved vegetable mix.
Mix thoroughly and add grated Parmesan. Cover the pan and simmer for 8–10 minutes to meld the flavors. Adjust seasoning and add a splash of water if needed.
Finally, add the crispy potatoes to the pan and gently fold them in until evenly combined. Top with mozzarella cheese and fresh parsley, cover until the cheese melts, then serve and ENJOY!

mealprep::garlic shrimp fried rice.name: Garlic Shrimp Fried Rice
ingredients: 15g (0.7oz) Reduced Fat Butter (for cooking) Yum Sauce
steps: Wash and devein the shrimp. In a bowl, marinate them with soy sauce, black pepper, garlic & onion powder, and chilli flakes. Toss until evenly coated, then set aside.
Prepare the yum yum sauce by combining light mayo, ketchup, sriracha, rice vinegar, ginger powder, and paprika. Mix until completely smooth. (You can bottle and store this sauce for later use)
Heat a pan over medium heat and add a little light butter. Cook the marinated raw shrimp for 1–2 minutes per side until golden and juicy, then set aside.
In the same pan, add chopped garlic and green onion; sauté for 2 minutes. Then add peas and carrots, cooking for another 2 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Push the vegetables to one side of the pan, add the whisked eggs, and stir continuously until scrambled. Once the eggs are softly cooked, combine them with the vegetables.

Add your day-old cooked rice along with extra soy sauce, oyster sauce, and a dab of light butter. Toss well, then return the cooked shrimp and additional green onion to the pan. Mix until everything is evenly combined.
Give the dish a final toss, then divide into 4 servings. Drizzle generously with the yum yum sauce and serve. ENJOY!

mealprep::roasted creamy garlic chicken.name: Roasted creamy Garlic chicken & Broccoli
ingredients: 1.5 Tsp Black Pepper 40g (1.4oz) Grated Parmigiano Reggiano
5 Whole Garlic Cloves (to roast for the white sauce)
steps: Butterfly cut the chicken breasts and season both sides evenly with oregano, garlic powder, chilli flakes, paprika, and olive oil. Set aside to marinate.
Wash and chop the broccoli into small florets. Place on a tray and toss with chilli flakes, garlic powder, salt, pepper, grated Parmesan, thinly sliced red onion, and olive oil.
Arrange 5 whole garlic cloves in the center of the tray. Air fry or oven bake the broccoli for 15–16 minutes at 200°C (400°F).
While the broccoli cooks, heat a pan over medium heat and cook the seasoned chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until golden and juicy. Let the chicken rest, then cut it into cubes.

Remove the roasted garlic cloves from the tray. In a blender, combine yogurt, mayo, the roasted garlic, crumbled feta, honey, and lemon juice. Blend until completely smooth to create your white sauce.

Layer the broccoli on a serving dish, top with the chicken cubes, and drizzle with the white sauce. Gently mix with two large spoons until well combined. Garnish with crushed walnuts and serve. ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy lemon garlic shrimp lin.name: creamy Lemon Garlic Shrimp Linguine
ingredients: 230g (8.1oz) Uncooked Linguine Pasta (or pasta of choice)
steps: Marinate the raw shrimp by mixing with minced garlic, Italian herbs, chilli flakes, smoked paprika, salt, lemon juice, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly and set aside.
Cook your linguine (or pasta of your choice) in boiling water for 7-8 minutes until al dente. Reserve some pasta water for the sauce, then drain the pasta.

Heat a pan over medium heat and add a little light butter. Sauté the marinated shrimp for about 2 minutes per side until golden. Lower the heat, then add a bit more butter, fresh parsley, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Toss to coat, then remove the shrimp from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan on low heat, combine milk, light cream cheese, Parmesan, extra seasoning, and some reserved pasta water. Stir continuously until the sauce thickens and becomes creamy.

Add the cooked pasta along with a handful of spinach, the cooked shrimp, extra parsley, and another squeeze of lemon juice. Toss everything until well combined, top with additional Parmesan, and serve. ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken tikka masala veggie ga.name: chicken Tikka Masala & Veggie Garlic Rice
ingredients: 20g (1oz) Reduced Fat Butter (for cooking) Vegetable Rice
150g Mixed Vegetables (Frozen or Fresh Carrot, Sweetcorn, Green Beans and Peas)
steps: Cut the chicken breast into cubes. In a mixing bowl, combine the chicken with yogurt, tikka masala spice mix, fresh minced garlic, and ginger. Mix thoroughly, then set aside to marinate.
Place the chicken cubes on a lined baking tray and air fry at 200°C for 20 minutes until golden and charred. Meanwhile, prepare the rice and sauce.

In a pot over medium heat, add butter and mixed vegetables. Sauté for 3–4 minutes until softened, then stir in fresh garlic, salt, turmeric, and cumin seeds. Cook for an additional 5 minutes until fragrant.
Add the washed, uncooked rice to the pot and stir to combine. Pour in water, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 8 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and fluffy.

In a separate pan over medium heat, melt butter and sauté chopped onions for 2 minutes. Stir in extra tikka masala spice mix and blended tomatoes, then cook for 8 minutes until the sauce thickens into a curry consistency.

Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in cream cheese until silky smooth and creamy. Fold in the cooked chicken, mixing well. Garnish with fresh coriander, then serve with the fragrant vegetable rice and ENJOY!

mealprep::cheeseburger potato bowls.name: cheeseburger Potato Bowls
ingredients: 80g (2.8oz) Light Cheddar Cheese (or any cheese) Salad
steps: Wash and slice the sweet potatoes into small cubes. Arrange them on a tray and season with salt, black pepper, garlic & onion powder, smoked paprika, and olive oil.
Toss the potatoes until evenly coated, then bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 25–30 minutes until golden crispy.

Meanwhile, prepare the special sauce. In a bowl, combine light mayo, ketchup, yellow mustard, onion powder, black pepper, smoked paprika, and pickle juice. Mix until smooth. (You can bottle and refrigerate the sauce for later use)
For the pickle salad, shred the lettuce and combine it in a bowl with chopped red onion, tomatoes, and pickles. Toss well and set aside.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and add butter. Cook the ground beef for 6–8 minutes until a deep, brown crust forms, stirring with a spatula to break up the meat. Season with salt and pepper.

Once the beef is cooked, add a little more butter to the pan and mix it through. Serve the beef (topped with a sprinkle of cheese, if desired) alongside the crispy sweet potatoes, fresh salad, and a generous drizzle of the special sauce. ENJOY!

mealprep::buttery garlic steak eggs.name: Buttery Garlic Steak & eggs
ingredients: Optional 2 Tsp Olive Oil (or cooking spray) Eggs
steps: Slice the steaks into 1-inch cubes then season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, parsley, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until evenly coated then set aside.

Cut the potatoes into small cubes and season with salt, black pepper, cumin, paprika, and garlic powder. Add a drizzle of olive oil or use cooking spray. Toss well, then spread them on a lined baking sheet. Oven-bake or air-fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20–22 minutes until golden and crispy.
For the spicy sauce, blend together yogurt, light mayo, chipotle chilli paste, salt, paprika, and garlic powder until smooth and creamy. Transfer to a squeezy bottle for easy storage.

Heat a pan over high heat, melt some butter, and sear the seasoned steak bites for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and charred. Lower the heat, add fresh minced garlic and fresh parsley, and toss until coated in the garlicky butter then set aside.
In the same pan over medium-low heat, add whisked eggs and stir continuously until scrambled and fluffy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Evenly distribute the steak bites, crispy potatoes, and scrambled eggs into 4 servings. Drizzle with the spicy sauce and ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken gyros bowls.name: chicken Gyros Bowls
steps: Slice the chicken breast cutlets and boneless thighs into halves. Marinate with minced garlic, Gyros spice mix, olive oil, yogurt, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Mix thoroughly until evenly coated.

Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers and place on a lined baking tray. Oven-bake or air-fry at 200°C (400°F) for 25 minutes until golden brown and juicy then set aside covered to rest and keep warm.

Cut the raw potatoes into fries and season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and a drizzle of olive oil. Toss until well coated, then oven- bake or air-fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20–25 minutes until crispy and golden brown. (If space allows, cook the chicken and fries together to save time)
For the tzatziki sauce, mix yogurt, grated cucumber, a pinch of salt, and lemon juice in a bowl. Stir until smooth and creamy, then refrigerate until ready to serve.
To prepare the garlic naan, combine flour, yogurt, salt, garlic powder, and baking powder in a bowl. Mix gently until crumbly, then knead into a smooth dough ball.

Divide the dough into four equal smaller balls, then roll each one out as thin as possible without tearing. Melt a little butter in a pan over medium-low heat and cook the naan for 2–3 minutes per side until golden and fully cooked through.
Roughly chop the cooked chicken into bite-sized cubes. Serve with crispy fries, warm garlic naan, a fresh side salad, and creamy tzatziki sauce. ENJOY!

mealprep::golden steak fried rice.name: Golden Steak Fried Rice
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, thinly sliced
4 Whole Eggs (separate egg yolks from egg whites)
steps: Slice the beef into thin strips and place in a mixing bowl. Marinate with black pepper, light and dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, and baking soda. Mix thoroughly until velvety and well combined then set aside.
Separate the egg yolks from the whites. In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice with the yolks, mixing until every grain is coated in a golden yellow hue.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat and melt butter. Cook the marinated beef strips for 2–3 minutes per side until a crispy crust forms. Avoid overcrowding the pan to ensure even browning. Once cooked, set the beef aside.
In the same pan on medium-low heat, add the white parts of the green onion along with minced garlic. Sauté for 3–4 minutes until fragrant.
Push the sautéed onions and garlic to one side of the pan, then add the egg whites. Stir continuously until scrambled, then mix them with the onions.

Add the golden-coated rice to the pan, along with extra soy sauce, the green parts of the green onion, a small knob of butter, and the cooked beef. Give everything a thoroug mix until well combined and flavourful.
Serve and distribute evenly into 4 portions, add a drizzle of sriracha and your high protein meals are ready to enjoy!

mealprep::mongolian beef noodles.name: Mongolian Beef Noodles
ingredients: 700g (25oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, cut into thin strips
100g (3.5oz) Red Onion, thinly sliced the Sauce
Garnish with Chilli Flakes & Sesame Seeds
steps: Slice the beef steaks into extra-thin strips. Marinate with light and dark soy sauce, garlic powder, black pepper, baking soda, olive oil, and cornflour. Mix thoroughly until velvety and well coated, then let it marinate for 15 minutes.
While the beef marinates, cook the egg noodles according to packet instructions or boil for 4–5 minutes until slightly underdone. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and spray with cooking oil. Cook the marinated beef for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy, then set aside. Repeat for the remaining beef.

In the same pan over medium heat, add chopped garlic, the white part of the green onion, and red onion. Cook for 4–5 minutes until fragrant, then pour in soy sauce, oyster sauce, and water. Bring to a light simmer, then stir in a cornstarch slurry.
Let the sauce thicken slightly, then add the cooked beef, the green part of the green onion, and the cooked noodles. Toss everything together until fully combined and coated in the rich sauce.
Garnish with chili flakes and sesame seeds, then divide into 4 equal servings. ENJOY!

mealprep::korean fried chicken.name: Korean Fried chicken
ingredients: 1 Tsp Chilli Flakes (omit for no spice)
210g (7.4oz) Uncooked White Rice/560g Cooked Rice (140g per serving)
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into bite-sized cubes, then season with garlic powder, black pepper, paprika, salt, and cornflour. Mix until evenly coated then set aside.
Cook your rice by bringing a pot of water to a boil and simmering for 8–10 minutes until fully cooked. I use basmati rice, but jasmine rice works too! Once done, set aside.
Heat a pan over medium heat, add olive oil, and cook the coated chicken for 4 minutes per side until golden brown with a crispy crust. Set aside and repeat for any remaining chicken.

In the same pan on low heat, add soy sauce mixed with water, a drizzle of honey, and gochujang paste. Stir gently until smooth and well combined. Let it simmer for 2–3 minutes until thickened, then return the crispy chicken and mix until fully coated in the spicy glaze.

For the cucumber salad, thinly slice cucumber and place in a bowl. Add light & dark soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic powder, chili flakes, and a generous amount of sesame seeds. Mix well until combined and fragrant. Serve alongside the glazed chicken and hot rice, then ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken biryani.name: chicken Biryani
ingredients: ½ tsp Salt or to taste
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into medium cubes. Marinate with minced garlic and ginger, garam masala, salt, paprika, cumin, turmeric, yogurt, lemon juice, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly then set aside to marinate for 30 minutes or overnight for best flavour.
Meanwhile, wash and cook the basmati rice, then thinly slice the red onions and chop up the tomatoes to use for later.

Spread the sliced onions on a lined baking tray, spray with a little olive oil, and toss to coat. Oven- bake or air-fry at 190°C (380°F) for 10–12 minutes until golden and crispy. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning.
While the onions cook, heat a pan over medium high heat, melt some butter, and cook the marinated chicken cubes for 4 minutes per side until golden brown and charred.
Once cooked, lower the heat, then add half of the crispy onions, the chopped tomatoes, freshly chopped coriander, extra salt to taste, and garam masala.

Give this a thorough mix until combined, cover, and cook on low heat for 10 minutes until the tomatoes break down into a thick curry sauce. Use a spatula to help mash them. This will smell incredibly fragrant!

Once the chicken curry is ready, start layering in the pan: chicken mix, rice, lemon slices, crispy onions, and chopped coriander. Repeat the layers once more and finsih the last layer with saffron infused water.
Cover for 5-10 mins on low heat to let the flavours intensify between the layers. Once done, you’re ready to serve and divide evenly into 4 portions and ENJOY! 185

mealprep::creamy spicy salmon ramen nood.name: creamy Spicy Salmon Ramen Noodles
ingredients: 2 Tsp Olive Oil (for cooking) Spicy Noodles
steps: Slice the skinless salmon into equal fillets/steaks. Season both sides with garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Rub the spices in evenly for maximum flavor absorption.
Heat a pan over medium heat, add a little olive oil, and cook the seasoned salmon for 4–5 minutes per side until beautifully golden brown and seared. Set aside to rest, keeping it warm.

While the salmon cooks, prepare your noodles according to package instructions or boil for 5 minutes until slightly underdone (they’ll finish cooking in the sauce). Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process, then set aside.

In the same pan over medium-low heat, sauté chopped garlic for 2 minutes until fragrant. Add chili garlic paste and light coconut milk, then season with salt, black pepper, and paprika. Simmer for 4–5 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add the cooked noodles, chopped green onion, and light & dark soy sauce. Mix gently until combined, then let it simmer for 3-4 minutes to thicken further.
Serve and divide the creamy noodles with the juicy seared salmon on top into 4 portions, and ENJOY!

mealprep::dynamite chicken loaded fries.name: Dynamite chicken Loaded Fries
ingredients: 2 Tsp Olive Oil or Cooking Spray
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes and season with oregano, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, soy sauce, and cornflour. Mix thoroughly until evenly coated.

Cut the potatoes into fries, then season with salt, pepper, oregano, paprika, garlic powder, and olive oil. Mix well, then spread on a lined baking tray then oven-bake or air fry for 20-22 minutes at 200°C (400°F) until golden and crispy.
For the dynamite sauce, combine mayo, chili garlic sauce, sriracha, sweet chili sauce, and lemon juice in a bowl. Mix until smooth and set aside.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add a little olive oil, and cook the seasoned chicken for 4 minutes per side until golden and a crispy crust forms then set aside. Repeat for the remaining chicken.
Mix in the cooked juicy chicken in the dynamite sauce until fully coated and indulgent.
Distribute the crispy fries and saucy dynamite chicken into 4 equal servings, garnish with green onion and sesame seeds, then ENJOY!

mealprep::crispy orange chicken noodles.name: crispy Orange chicken Noodles
ingredients: 2-3 Tsp Olive Oil (for cooking) Orange Sauce
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into bite-sized cubes. Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and cornflour. Mix thoroughly until well coated, then set aside.
Cook the egg noodles according to packet instructions. Once done, rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process, then set aside.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add olive oil, and cook the seasoned chicken for 4 minutes per side until golden and a crispy crust forms. Set aside and repeat with any remaining chicken.
In the same pan over medium heat, add chopped garlic, ginger, and chili flakes. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant, then stir in orange juice, soy sauce, honey, and orange zest.
Let the sauce bubble and reduce for 5 minutes on medium-low heat. Once it slightly thickens, add a cornstarch slurry (water mixed with cornstarch), stirring until the sauce further thickens.
Return the crispy chicken to the pan and coat it well in the sauce. Add the cooked noodles and chopped green onion, then mix everything until well combined and evenly coated.
Finally garnish with sesame seeds and chili flakes, divide into 4 portions, and ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy garlic cheesy steak pot.name: creamy Garlic cheesy Steak & Potatoes
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Topside/ Top Sirloin Beef, cubed
2 Tsp Mixed Herbs (Parsley, Oregano, Thyme, Rosemary)
steps: Slice the beef steaks into 1-inch cubes and place them in a mixing bowl. Add minced garlic, salt, black pepper, mixed herbs, smoked paprika, and olive oil. Mix well until evenly coated, then set aside.

Slice the potatoes into small cubes, then season with garlic powder, mixed herbs, smoked paprika, and olive oil. Toss to coat, then spread evenly on a lined baking tray. Bake in a preheated oven or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20–23 minutes until golden and crispy.

While the potatoes cook, heat a pan over medium- high heat, melt some butter, and sear the seasoned steak bites for 3 minutes per side until a golden- brown crust forms. Remove from the pan and set aside. Repeat for the remaining steak bites.

In the same pan over medium heat, add chopped onions, mushrooms, and cherry tomatoes. Cook for 5 minutes until softened, then lower the heat and stir in milk, cream cheese, and grated parmesan. Stir continuously for 3–4 minutes until the sauce is smooth, thick, and creamy.

Add a squeeze of lemon juice for tanginess, then return the steak bites and crispy potatoes to the pan. Stir everything together until fully incorporated. Sprinkle cheddar cheese and chili flakes, then serve and ENJOY!

mealprep::butter chicken shawarma rice b.name: Butter chicken Shawarma Rice Bowls
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Chicken Breast, cut into thin strips
Juice of ½ Lemon Chicken Sauce
1 Tsp each: Salt, Paprika, Turmeric Salad
½ Tsp each: Salt, Pepper, Garlic Powder
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into thin strips, then marinate with minced garlic, tomato paste, salt, pepper, onion powder, cinnamon, paprika, olive oil, and lemon juice. Mix thoroughly and let it marinate.
For the golden rice, bring a pot of water to a boil and add raw basmati rice with salt, turmeric, and paprika. Stir and cook for 8–10 minutes until fully cooked and fluffy.
Lay the marinated chicken flat on a lined sheet pan, ensuring an even layer, then oven bake or air fry at 190°C (375°F) for 12–15 minutes until golden brown and slightly charred.
While the chicken cooks, heat a pan over medium heat, melt some butter, and sauté sliced onions for 5 minutes until soft. Add fresh tomatoes, cashew nuts, and butter chicken seasoning.
Mix well and let it cook for 5–7 minutes on medium- low heat until the tomatoes break down. Lower the heat and stir in yogurt until fully combined.
Allow the mixture to cool, then blend for 3–4 minutes until completely smooth.
Reheat the sauce in a pan before serving, or store it in a squeeze bottle for easy use.
By now, the chicken shawarma should be done. Prepare the quick side salad, then serve everything evenly with golden rice and a generous drizzle of butter chicken sauce. Enjoy

mealprep::creamy chicken alfredo.name: creamy chicken Alfredo
ingredients: 1 Tsp each: Italian Herbs, Salt, Black Pepper
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into thinner fillets, then season both sides with Italian herbs, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Rub the seasoning in evenly, then set aside.
Cook the pasta. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil, add the spaghetti pasta, and cook for 8 minutes until al dente. Reserve some pasta water, then drain the rest.
Prepare the Alfredo sauce by blending milk, cream cheese, parmesan, Italian herbs, salt, pepper, and the reserved pasta water until smooth. Then set aside.
Heat a pan over medium heat with a little olive oil. Cook the seasoned chicken for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown. Keep an eye on the spices to prevent burning. Once done, set aside.

In the same pan, sauté chopped garlic and onion over medium heat for 2–3 minutes until fragrant. Lower the heat, then pour in the blended Alfredo sauce. Stir continuously with a whisk until thick and creamy.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the sauce, mixing gently until fully coated. Serve with the sliced chicken, garnish with parsley, and drizzle with extra sauce. Enjoy!

mealprep::chicken chow mein noodles.name: chicken chow Mein Noodles
ingredients: 700g (25oz) Raw Chicken Breast, cut into thin strips
20g (1oz) Dark Soy Sauce Mein Noodles + Sauce
220g (7.8oz) Uncooked Egg Noodles (or noodles of choice)
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into thin strips and place minutes until golden brown and slightly charred. Set them in a mixing bowl. Marinate with paprika, black aside and repeat if needed. pepper, garlic powder, and light & dark soy sauce.
Mix thoroughly and let sit for a few minutes to In the same pan over medium-high heat, stir-fry deepen the flavour. the sliced onion, bell pepper, and green onion

for 5 minutes till fragrant. Add minced garlic, soy Slice the red onion, bell pepper, and green sauce, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, and the cooked onion. Cook the noodles according to package noodles. Mix gently but thoroughly until well instructions—I boiled and simmered them in water combined. for 4–5 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, and
set aside. Finally add the cooked chicken and bean sprouts,
mix again, then garnish with sesame seeds and chili Heat a pan over medium heat, add cooking spray or flakes. Distribute into 4 servings and ENJOY! olive oil, and cook the marinated chicken for 6-8

mealprep::chipotle steak bowls.name: chipotle Steak Bowls
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef 1 Inch Thick Steaks
½ Tsp each: Garlic Powder, Salt, Black Pepper de Gallo
Juice of ½ or 1 Lime Lime Rice
210g (7.4oz) Uncooked Basmati Rice (or rice of choice)
steps: Season both sides of the steaks evenly with garlic powder, parsley, paprika, cumin, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Rub the spices in until evenly coated and the color deepens then set aside.
Make the avocado sauce by blending avocado, yogurt, fresh coriander, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper until smooth and creamy. Transfer to a squeezy bottle for easy storage.
For the coriander lime rice, bring water to a boil in a pot, add the raw basmati rice, then simmer and cook for 8–10 minutes until fully cooked and fluffy.
Once cooked, stir in a generous handful of chopped coriander, fresh lime juice, and salt to taste. Gently mix until well combined and fluffed up, then set aside.

Heat a pan over medium heat, add a little olive oil or butter and cook the marinated steaks for 4 minutes per side, adjusting based on thickness. They should come out seared and golden brown. Let them rest for 4–5 minutes before slicing into juicy cubes.

Make the pico salsa by combining chopped tomato, red onion, red and green bell peppers, freshly chopped coriander, salt, pepper, and lime juice. Mix until well incorporated. Store this separately for easier reheating.

Make the pico salsa by combining chopped tomato, red onion, red and green bell peppers, freshly chopped coriander, salt, pepper, and lime juice. Mix until well incorporated. Store this separately for easier reheating.

mealprep::korean beef fried rice.name: Korean Beef Fried Rice
ingredients: 200g (7oz) Uncooked White Rice or 530g Leftover Cooked Rice
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine light & dark soy sauce, honey, minced garlic and gochujang. Mix until smooth and well combined. You will use this sauce for the both the beef and rice.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add olive oil, then cook the minced beef for 6 minutes until fully browned with no pink bits remaining. Use a spatula to break up the meat while it cooks.

Lower the heat to medium, add half of the prepared Korean sauce along with chopped green onions and sesame seeds. Stir well and cook for another 4–5 minutes until the beef turns golden and crispy then set aside.
In the same pan over medium heat, add chopped onions and grated carrots. Cook for 5 minutes until softened, then push them to one side of the pan and add the whisked eggs.
Continuously stir the eggs until scrambled, then mix them with the vegetables until well incorporated.
Add your leftover cooked rice to the pan along with the remaining Korean sauce and more chopped green onions. Stir thoroughly until every grain of rice is evenly coated.
Return the cooked crispy beef to the pan and gently mix until fully incorporated. Garnish with sesame seeds.

For the Korean spicy sauce, mix together light mayo, gochujang, garlic powder, and rice vinegar until smooth. Serve the beef fried rice evenly into 4 portions with a drizzle of spicy Korean sauce and ENJOY!

mealprep::honey crispy chicken mac n che.name: Honey crispy chicken Mac n cheese
ingredients: ½ Tsp each: Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Smoked Paprika, Salt, Black Pepper
200g (7oz) Uncooked Macaroni Pasta (or pasta of choice)
½ Tsp each: Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Smoked Paprika, Salt, Black Pepper
steps: Slice the chicken into bite-sized pieces, then season with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, pepper, hot sauce and whisked eggs. Mix well until evenly coated in the wet batter. Set up the crushed cornflakes coating station.
While the chicken marinates, cook the macaroni in boiling water according to packet instructions. Reserve some pasta water before draining, then set aside.
Drop and coat each chicken piece in the crushed cornflakes station. Ensure every piece is evenly coated for a crispy finish.

Place the coated chicken bites on a lined baking tray or in the air fryer basket. Spray lightly with cooking spray, then oven bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 18–20 minutes, until crispy and golden.
In a blender, combine milk, cream cheese, grated cheddar, and the same spices used for the chicken. Blend until smooth and creamy.

Pour the blended sauce into a pan over medium- low heat. Stir for 2–3 minutes until slightly thickened, then add the cooked macaroni along with some reserved pasta water to adjust consistency. Mix well until the pasta is creamy and fully coated. Garnish with parsley.
Make the sticky glaze by combining honey, hot sauce and squeeze of lemon juice. Drizzle over the crispy chicken bites till well coated.

mealprep::beef bulgogi kimchi fried rice.name: Beef Bulgogi Kimchi Fried Rice
ingredients: 900g (32oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, cut into thin strips
steps: Slice your beef into thin strips. Place in a mixing bowl and marinate with light and dark soy sauce, black pepper, chili flakes, chili powder, garlic paste, ginger paste, sesame seeds, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until all pieces are well coated, then set aside to marinate while you prep other ingredients.

Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add a small amount of light butter, and sear the marinated beef for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and slightly crispy. Do not overcrowd the pan—work in batches to ensure the beef sears properly. Once cooked, set the beef aside.

In the same pan on medium heat, add a bit more butter if needed, then sauté chopped garlic, gochujang paste, chopped green onion (white parts), and grated carrots. Stir-fry for 2–3 minutes until fragrant and the vegetables have softened slightly.

Add your cooked jasmine rice to the pan and stir well, ensuring each grain gets coated in the gochujang mixture. Cook for 2–3 minutes, mixing until the rice turns a vibrant orange color and is heated through.

To the rice, add kimchi juice, chopped kimchi, additional green onion (green parts), and a splash of soy sauce. Mix thoroughly till well incorporated, and cook for another 2–3 minutes so the flavors meld.
Finally, add the cooked beef back into the pan. Gently mix everything together. Let it heat through for another 1–2 minutes. Garnish with extra sesame seeds, divide into 4 servings, and ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy garlic pepper steak.name: creamy Garlic Pepper Steak
steps: Slice your beef steaks into thin strips, then season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika, chilli flakes, and olive oil. Mix well and marinate for at least 15 minutes (or longer for deeper flavor).

Prepare the seasoned rice. In a pot, add water and raw rice, bring to a boil, then lower to a simmer and cook for 8–10 minutes until fully cooked. Once done, season with mixed herbs, salt, and chilli flakes. Stir to combine until fragrant and set aside.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add light butter, and cook the marinated beef for 5–6 minutes until golden brown and juicy. Avoid overcrowding the pan for best sear.
Lower the heat to medium-low, then add milk, cream cheese, grated parmesan, extra salt, black pepper, and garlic powder.
Stir everything together until smooth and creamy. Add a large handful of spinach and mix it in. Let it simmer for 5–8 minutes until the sauce thickens and spinach is wilted.
Once the sauce is ready, serve with the seasoned rice, distribute evenly into 4 portions, and ENJOY your high-protein meals!

mealprep::mexican style chicken rice.name: Mexican Style chicken & Rice
ingredients: 1 Tsp each: Oregano, Garlic Powder, Paprika, Cumin, Chilli Powder, Salt, Black Pepper
steps: Slice your chicken breasts into bite-sized cubes. Season generously with oregano, garlic powder, paprika, cumin, chili powder, salt, black pepper, and a squeeze of lime juice. Mix thoroughly until every piece is well coated - if the color doesn’t deepen, add more seasoning.

Heat a pan over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil or cooking spray. Cook the seasoned chicken for about 7–8 minutes, turning occasionally until golden brown and fully cooked through. Once done, remove from the pan and set aside.

In the same pan, add chopped onions and bell peppers. Sauté for 2–3 minutes until slightly soft. Then add black beans (drained and rinsed), uncooked washed rice, and all the extra seasoning for added flavor.

Toast the rice with the veggies and beans for 1–2 minutes. Pour in canned chopped tomatoes and water (enough to cover the rice by about 1 inch). Stir well and bring to a gentle boil. Cover with a lid and simmer on low heat for 12–15 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and fluffy. Stir halfway through to ensure even cooking and prevent sticking.
Once the rice is cooked, add the cooked chicken back in, along with chopped fresh coriander. Mix everything thoroughly until well combined. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Divide evenly into 4 portions, garnish with extra coriander or lime wedges if desired, and ENJOY!

mealprep::peri peri chicken mac n cheese.name: Peri Peri chicken Mac n cheese
ingredients: 400g (14oz) Raw Chicken Thighs, skinless fat trimmed
240g Uncooked/540g Cooked Macaroni Peri Peri Cheese Sauce
1 Tsp each: Salt, Black Pepper, Onion Powder, Oregano, Paprika
steps: Place your chicken breasts and thighs in a large dish, then marinate with minced garlic, oregano, paprika, onion powder, salt, pepper, and Nando’s marinade. Mix well until the color changes and set aside to marinate.

While the chicken marinates, bring a pot of water to a boil. Add the raw macaroni pasta and boil for 7–8 minutes until al dente. Reserve some pasta water before draining the rest, then set pasta aside.
Heat a pan over medium heat, add a little oil or cooking spray, and cook the marinated chicken for 5 minutes on each side until golden brown and juicy. Repeat in batches if needed.
Once all the chicken is cooked, set aside to rest, then chop into cubes just before serving with the mac n cheese.

In the same pan on medium heat, add chopped onions and tomato paste. Cook for about 5 minutes until softened. Then lower the heat and add milk, cream cheese, grated cheddar, extra seasonings, peri-peri hot sauce, and the reserved pasta water. Stir thoroughly until everything melts into a smooth, creamy cheese sauce.
Once the sauce is thick and creamy, add the cooked macaroni and mix well to coat. Serve evenly with the cubed chicken, and your high-protein meal prep is ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken shawarma crispy fries.name: chicken Shawarma & crispy Fries
ingredients: 1 Tsp each: Salt, Black Pepper, Oregano, Paprika
steps: Start by slicing the chicken breasts into butterflied halves. Place in a bowl and marinate with fresh garlic, smoked paprika, onion powder, cinnamon, cumin, salt, pepper, olive oil, tomato paste, and lemon juice. Mix thoroughly until every piece is well coated, then set aside to marinate.

While the chicken is marinating, slice the potatoes into fries and lay them flat on a lined baking tray. Season with salt, pepper, oregano, paprika, and olive oil, then toss to coat evenly. Bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 20–25 minutes until golden and crispy, flipping halfway if needed.
Take a loaf tin and layer the marinated chicken slices tightly, stacking them to fill the tin completely. Bake for 25 minutes at 200°C (400°F) until cooked through and juicy with a golden top.
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for 5 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare the garlic sauce and crunchy slaw using the ingredients listed (optional: make these ahead for quicker assembly).
After resting, thinly slice the stacked chicken to reveal shawarma-style strips. Serve alongside the crispy fries, pickles, slaw, and drizzle generously with garlic sauce. Enjoy!

mealprep::crispy sticky orange chicken.name: crispy Sticky Orange chicken
ingredients: 1/2 or 1 Tsp Chilli Flakes (omit for less heat)
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into cubes, season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and cornflour. Mix well until evenly coated — using a ziplock bag makes this easier and mess-free.
In a bowl, combine orange juice, soy sauce, honey, and orange zest — set this sauce aside for later.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add olive oil or butter, and cook the coated chicken for 4–5 minutes on each side until golden brown and crisp. Set aside when cooked.

In the same pan on medium heat, add chopped garlic, ginger, and chili flakes. Sauté for 1–2 minutes until fragrant, then pour in the orange sauce. Let it bubble and reduce for 3–4 minutes, then stir in a cornstarch slurry to thicken.
Once slighty thickened, return the crispy chicken to the sauce, mixing until well coated and glossy.
Meanwhile, cook your rice in a pot of water. Once cooked, serve everything evenly into portions, garnish with orange slices, green onions, and sesame seeds — then ENJOY!

mealprep::creamy steak fajita pasta.name: creamy Steak Fajita Pasta
ingredients: 700g (25oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, cut into steaks
20g Reduced Fat Butter (for cooking) Fajita Sauce
1 Tsp each: Garlic Powder, Oregano, Salt, Chilli Flakes
Extra Salt and Seasoning, to taste if needed
steps: Slice your beef into 1-inch thick steaks, then season both sides generously with garlic powder, chili powder, salt, black pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil. Rub the spices in thoroughly until the steaks are fully coated. Set aside to marinate while you prep the rest.

Bring a pot of water to a boil and add penne pasta. Cook for 7–8 minutes until al dente. Before draining, reserve about ½ cup of pasta water to help adjust the sauce later. Drain and set the pasta aside.

In a blender cup, add milk, cream cheese, grated parmesan, garlic powder, oregano, salt, chili powder, and some of the reserved pasta water. Blend until smooth and creamy. Set aside—this will be the base of your sauce.

Get a large pan on medium-high heat, add a bit of olive oil or butter, then cook the marinated steaks for 3–4 minutes on each side until a nice golden- brown crust forms. Once cooked, set the steaks aside to rest for 5 minutes—this helps retain the juices when you slice them later.
In the same pan on medium heat, add sliced onions and bell peppers. Cook for about 5 minutes until soft and fragrant, scraping up any leftover steak bits for added flavor.

Lower the heat slightly to medium-low and pour in the creamy sauce from the blender. Let it simmer for 3–4 minutes, stirring gently, until it starts to thicken. Then add your cooked penne pasta and toss everything together until each piece of pasta is well coated and creamy.

Finally, slice the rested steaks into thin strips. Serve the creamy fajita pasta evenly into 4 portions, top each serving with sliced steak, and garnish with fresh parsley. Your high-protein meal is ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::sticky honey garlic chicken.name: Sticky Honey Garlic chicken
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into bite-sized cubes. Add minced garlic, paprika, black pepper, sriracha, and soy sauce. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is fully coatet and let this marinate for enhanced flavour.

Heat a pan on medium heat, add olive oil, and cook the marinated chicken for about 8 minutes, stirring occasionally until golden and cooked through. Lower the heat completely, drizzle in honey, and stir well to glaze the chicken. Once coated, set the chicken aside.
In the same pan, add minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Then add sliced red onion and bell pepper, cooking for 5 minutes until softened.

Add cooked rice to the pan, followed by soy sauce, honey, and sesame oil. Stir well until the rice is evenly coated and turns a darker color. Return the chicken to the pan along with chopped green onion, and mix everything together.
Garnish with sesame seeds. Distribute into 4 equal portions for meal prep or serve immediately and ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken pad thai noodles.name: chicken Pad Thai Noodles
ingredients: 2 Tsp Olive Oil (for cooking) Thai Sauce
Squeeze of Lime Juice to finish
steps: Slice the chicken breasts into small cubes and place in a bowl. Add soy sauce, black pepper, garlic, and chili flakes. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is evenly coated — the color should deepen. Set aside to marinate while you prep the rest.
In a separate bowl, make the Pad Thai sauce by combining soy sauce, tamarind paste, honey, fish sauce, sriracha, and lime juice. Whisk until fully combined and smooth, then set aside for later.
Heat a pan over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Cook the marinated chicken for 3–4 minutes on each side until golden brown and cooked through. Once done, remove and set aside.

In the same pan, add a little more oil if needed. Add freshly chopped garlic, onion, and the white part of green onion. Sauté for 2–3 minutes until soft and fragrant. Push the veg to one side of the pan, crack in 3 eggs, and scramble them, stirring constantly until cooked.

Once the eggs are scrambled, mix them into the cooked vegetables. Push everything to one side of the pan and add your cooked rice noodles along with the sauce you made earlier. Toss everything together until the noodles are fully coated in sauce and take on a deep golden color.

Add the cooked chicken back to the pan along with green onion and bean sprouts. Give everything a final mix until well combined and heated through. Garnish with crushed peanuts, extra chili flakes, and a squeeze of lime juice, then serve and ENJOY!

mealprep::cheesy garlic steak sweet pota.name: cheesy Garlic Steak & Sweet Potato Bites
ingredients: 800g (28oz) Raw Topside/Top Sirloin Beef, 1 inch cubed
steps: Start by slicing your steaks into bite-sized cubes. Place in a mixing bowl, add minced garlic, salt, black pepper, parsley, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until every piece is well coated, then set aside to marinate while you prep the potatoes.

Slice your sweet potatoes into small, even cubes for faster and even cooking. Add to a bowl, season with salt, pepper, paprika, parsley, minced garlic, and a drizzle of olive oil (optional for extra crispiness). Toss until all pieces are well coated.
Lay the seasoned sweet potatoes out evenly on a lined sheet pan without overcrowding. Bake or air fry at 200°C (400°F) for 17–19 minutes, shaking halfway through, until golden and crispy.

While the potatoes cook, heat a pan over medium- high heat, add light butter, and cook the steak bites for 2–3 minutes per side until a deep golden- brown crust forms. Avoid overcrowding to ensure a good sear — cook in batches if needed.
Once all steak bites are done, lower the heat, add a handful of fresh parsley, and stir well to coat in the butter and herbs.

Add the crispy sweet potatoes to the steak, toss everything together gently, then sprinkle grated cheddar over the top. Cover the pan with a lid for 1–2 minutes to melt the cheese. Finally, distribute evenly into 4 portions and ENJOY!

mealprep::chicken teriyaki fried rice.name: chicken Teriyaki Fried Rice
ingredients: 200g (7oz) Uncooked White Rice/500g (17.5oz) Cooked Rice (leftover/day old rice is best)
steps: Slice the chicken breast into bite-sized cubes, then add soy sauce, all-purpose seasoning, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until well coated and set aside to marinate.
In a separate bowl, make the teriyaki sauce by combining soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, cornflour (or cornstarch), and water. Mix until smooth and set aside for later.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add cooking spray or a little olive oil, add the beaten eggs then scramble for 2–3 minutes until fluffy. Remove from the pan and set aside.

In the same pan on medium high heat, cook the marinated chicken for about 7–8 minutes until golden brown. lower the heat then pour in the teriyaki sauce, mix and let it bubble and thicken for 2–3 minutes until all pieces are well coated, then set the chicken aside.

Using the same pan, add a bit more oil if needed, then sauté chopped onions, ginger, carrots, peas, and bean sprouts for 5–8 minutes until soft and fragrant. Add cooked white rice, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Stir well to combine.

Add the teriyaki chicken, scrambled eggs, and green onions to the fried rice mixture. Mix everything thoroughly until well combined. Garnish with sesame seeds and distriute into 4 even portions and ENJOY!

mealprep::cheesy beef loaded fries.name: cheesy Beef Loaded Fries
ingredients: 1/2 Tsp each: Salt, Garlic and Onion Powder
Squeeze of Lime Juice Spice Mix (1 Serving)
steps: Slice the potatoes into fries, then season with salt, oregano, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cumin, and olive oil. Toss until evenly coated, then oven bake or air fry for about 20 minutes at 200°C (400°F) until golden and crispy then set aside.
For the garlic sauce, combine yoghurt, mayo, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and squeeze of lime juice in a bowl. Mix until smooth and creamy, then set aside.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat, add light butter and chopped onions. Sauté for about 8 minutes until soft and caramelised, then remove and set aside.

In the same pan, cook the minced beef on medium- high heat for 6 minutes until browned. Add 1 serving taco spice mix and the caramelised onions. Cook for another 4–5 minutes until well combined and the beef deepens in color.
Lower the heat, sprinkle over cheddar cheese, and cover the pan for a minute or two until the cheese melts.

Divide the crispy fries into portions, top with the cheesy taco beef, freshly chopped red onion, jalapenos, tomatoes, fresh corinader then drizzle generously with garlic sauce and sriracha, and ENJOY!

mealprep::cheesy bbq chicken crispy pota.name: cheesy BBQ chicken & crispy Potatoes
ingredients: 2 Tsp Olive Oil Cooking & Final Glaze
2 Tsp Olive Oil or Cooking Spray
steps: Start by slicing your chicken breasts into bite- sized cubes. Season with salt, oregano, chili flakes, onion powder, paprika, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until well coated, then set aside to marinate.
Chop your potatoes into small cubes. Season with salt, garlic powder, paprika, oregano, and olive oil. Toss until every piece is evenly coated.
Spread the seasoned potatoes out on a lined sheet pan in a single layer. Oven bake at 200°C (400°F) for 20 minutes or air fry for 18 minutes until golden and crispy.
Heat a pan over medium heat, melt a bit of butter, then cook the marinated chicken for 6–8 minutes until golden brown and cooked through.
Once the chicken is cooked, lower the heat, then stir in extra butter, minced garlic, chopped parsley, and drizzle of BBQ sauce. Using a spatula, mix until the chicken is well coated and glazed.
Add the crispy potatoes to the pan and mix with the chicken till well incorporated. Sprinkle over shredded mozzarella, cover with a lid on low heat, and let it melt for 2–3 minutes.
Once the cheese is melted and gooey, drizzle with extra BBQ sauce, divide into four equal portions, and your high protein meal prep is ready to ENJOY!

mealprep::one pan tomato chicken.name: One-Pan Tomato chicken
ingredients: 1/2 Tsp each: Garlic Powder, Paprika, Dried Basil, Salt, Black Pepper
200g (7oz) Uncooked Basmati Rice/540g Cooked Rice (140g per serving)
steps: Slice your chicken breasts into thinner halves. Season both sides with garlic powder, parsley, paprika, dried basil, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Rub the seasoning in until evenly coated.
Heat a pan over medium heat, then cook the seasoned chicken for 4–5 minutes on the first side until golden and slightly charred. Flip and cook the other side for 2–3 minutes.
Pour in canned chopped tomatoes around the chicken along with thinly sliced red onion. Sprinkle in a bit more seasoning for extra flavour.
Gently mix the onions and tomatoes together, spreading evenly around the chicken. Cover with a lid and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring halfway to blend the flavours.
Meanwhile, boil your rice in a pot of water until cooked. Once done, serve alongside the saucy chicken. Garnish with fresh parsley and ENJOY!

mealprep::beef chilli.name: Beef chilli
ingredients: Handful Green Onion Garnish Lime Rice
250g (8.8oz) Raw Basmati or Long Grain Rice
steps: Start by prepping all your vegetables- finely chop the onion, red and green bell peppers, and mince the garlic. Wash your rice and set it aside for later.
Now cook all your vegetables in a pan.
In the same pan, add the ground beef and cook over medium-high heat for 8–10 minutes until fully browned and no pink remains. Use a spatula to break up the meat continuously for an even texture.

Add the cooked veggies back into the pan along with tomato paste, salt, oregano, onion powder, smoked paprika, cumin, and chili powder. Stir well for 1–2 minutes until the spices are toasted and the meat turns a rich golden brown.

Lower the heat to medium-low, then stir in the canned chopped tomatoes, chopped pickled jalapeños, and 300-400ml of beef broth . Mix everything thoroughly, then cover with a lid and let it simmer on low heat for 15–20 minutes.

While the chili simmers, prepare the rice. In a separate pot, bring water to a boil, add the raw rice, and simmer on low heat for 8–10 minutes until fully cooked and fluffy. Drain any excess water, then stir in a big handful of chopped coriander, the juice of one lime, and salt to taste. Mix well and set aside.

After the chili has simmered for 15 minutes, remove the lid and add the red kidney beans. Stir to combine, then simmer uncovered for another 5 minutes. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of beef broth or water to adjust consistency.

To serve, divide the coriander lime rice evenly into 5 containers. Top with the beef chili, then garnish with shredded cheese, chopped coriander, and green onion. Your high-protein meals are ready to ENJOY! 235

mealprep::korean bbq chicken.name: Korean BBQ chicken
ingredients: 35g (1.3oz / 5 Tbsp) Sesame Seeds Spicy Sauce batch, store in bottle)
steps: MARINATE THE CHICKEN: Trim any excess fat from your boneless chicken thighs, then place them in a large mixing bowl. Add black pepper, garlic paste, ginger paste, gochujang, sriracha, sesame seeds, and light soy sauce. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is fully coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate for at least 1 hour, or overnight for more intense flavour. You can also freeze the raw marinated chicken and defrost it whenever you need a quick protein base for the week.

COOK THE CHICKEN: When ready to cook, spread the marinated chicken thighs evenly in a single layer on a lined sheet pan (baking paper/parchment paper). Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 16-18 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through, juicy, and has a light golden char on the edges.

REST THE CHICKEN: ( K E E P W H O L E F O R S TO R AG E ) Let the chicken thighs rest for 3-4 minutes, then leave them whole for storage. Keeping the thighs intact helps retain their natural juices and prevents them from drying out over the week. Make sure to pack all the resting juices from the sheet pan together with the chicken when storing. These juices are essential for extra flavour and help keep the chicken moist during storage and reheating, ensuring nothing gets wasted.

STORE FOR THE WEEK: Allow the chicken to cool completely, then store the whole pieces in airtight glass containers and refrigerate for the week. Slice or cube the chicken only when serving to maintain maximum tenderness and juiciness. ENJOY!

mealprep::bang bang chicken.name: Bang Bang chicken
ingredients: 1500g (53oz / 3.3lbs) Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, cubed
60g (2.1oz / 4 Tbsp) Reduced Sugar Sweet Chilli Sauce
steps: Slice the boneless, skinless chicken thighs into bite-sized cubes. Place them in a large bowl and season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, ginger powder, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is evenly coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate.

To make the bang bang sauce, combine light mayo, yogurt, sweet chilli sauce, sriracha, rice vinegar, and freshly chopped parsley in a bowl. Mix until smooth. Separate a small amount of this sauce into another bowl - this will be used to baste the chicken during cooking.

Spread the marinated chicken cubes in a single layer on a lined sheet pan. Use a brush to lightly coat each piece with the reserved bang bang sauce. Bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 22-25 minutes until golden and slightly crispy. Once cooked, while the chicken is still hot, brush over a final layer of bang bang sauce to create a glossy, flavour-packed glaze.

Allow the chicken to cool slightly, then store the chicken whole as cubes in airtight containers, making sure to include any tray juices for extra flavour and moisture. Keep a portion of the bang bang sauce stored separately in a small bottle or airtight container in the fridge. This is ideal for adding extra sauce when serving or when reheating the chicken throughout the week for maximum flavour and moisture retention, ENJOY! BEST WAYS TO SERVE AND PAIR THIS PROTEIN This Bang Bang Chicken works extremely well in simple, easy meal prep combinations, for example: – With jasmine or coconut rice and cucumber – With crispy roasted potatoes and a side salad – In a tortilla wrap with lettuce and sweetcorn – Mango or pineapple salsa for sweetness and freshness SAUCE PAIRING SUGGESTIONS This chicken also pairs well with: – Extra bang bang sauce when serving – Chilli-lime mayo – Yogurt and lemon mixed together

mealprep::garlic herb cajun chicken.name: Garlic Herb cajun chicken
steps: Get your boneless, skinless chicken thighs and place them in a large bowl. Season with salt, Italian herbs, chilli flakes, parsley, garlic powder, onion powder and paprika, then drizzle over the olive oil. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is well coated and fully combined.
Place all the chicken thighs flat and evenly spread on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 22-25 minutes. The chicken should come out golden and charred on the edges.

Once cooked, let the chicken rest for 4-5 minutes. For best meal prep results, store the chicken whole to help retain juices and prevent drying out over the week. Make sure to include any tray juices when storing for extra flavour and moisture. Slice into cubes only when serving, or leave whole depending on preference. Store in large airtight glass containers and enjoy a delicious protein source ready for the week, ENJOY! BEST WAYS TO SERVE AND PAIR THIS PROTEIN This Garlic Herb Cajun Chicken works extremely well in simple, flavourful combinations, for example: – With cajun butter rice and sweetcorn – With crispy roasted potatoes and a simple coleslaw – In a warm tortilla wrap with lettuce and pickles SAUCE PAIRING SUGGESTIONS This chicken also pairs well with: – Lemon garlic yogurt dip – Spicy mayo (light mayo mixed with a little hot sauce) – Honey mustard yogurt sauce

mealprep::tandoori chicken.name: Tandoori chicken
ingredients: 1500g (53oz / 3.3lbs) Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, trim excess fat
4 Tsp Tandoori Masala (salt included) Brand: Shaan
steps: Trim any excess fat from the boneless chicken thighs, then place them in a large mixing bowl. Season with tandoori masala, black pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, cumin, and coriander powder, then add olive oil, yogurt, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Mix well until fully combined and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate - the longer it rests, the more intense the flavour will be.

Line a sheet pan with parchment paper. Spread the marinated chicken thighs in a single layer and sprinkle a little extra tandoori seasoning on top. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 20-25 minutes, until cooked through, golden and crispy on the edges. Once the chicken is cooked, set it aside to rest.

For best meal prep results, keep the chicken whole when storing to retain juiciness. Make sure to include any resting juices from the sheet pan with the chicken for extra flavour and moisture. Slice into cubes only when serving for maximum tenderness. ENJOY! BEST WAYS TO SERVE AND PAIR THIS PROTEIN This Tandoori Chicken works extremely well in simple, flavourful combinations, for example: – With lemon herb rice – With roasted potatoes or air fried chips/fries and a simple creamy coleslaw or salad – In a warm wrap with lettuce and onions SAUCE PAIRING SUGGESTIONS This chicken also pairs well with: – Mint yogurt sauce – Garlic yogurt sauce – Mango chutney for sweetness

mealprep::honey chipotle chicken.name: Honey chipotle chicken
ingredients: 100g (3.5oz) Chipotle Paste or Chipotle Peppers in Adobo
300g (7oz) Fresh Pineapple, chopped into chunks Sauce batch, store in bottle)
steps: Place the boneless, skinless chicken thighs in a bowl and season with salt, oregano, garlic powder, smoked paprika, cumin, chipotle paste, honey, and fresh lime juice. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is fully coated and deeply coloured, then set aside to marinate.

Lay the marinated chicken thighs evenly on a lined sheet pan, then scatter pineapple chunks around the chicken. This allows the pineapple to caramelise and soften while releasing extra sweetness and moisture that enhances the flavour of the chicken. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 20-23 minutes, or until golden, juicy, and slightly charred at the edges, with the pineapple lightly caramelised.

While the chicken rests, make the pineapple chipotle sauce. In a blender cup add yogurt, mayo, chipotle paste, pineapple chunks, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and lime juice. Blend for 30-60 seconds until completely smooth and creamy.

Bottle up the sauce for the week (great for wraps and bowls). For meal prep, keep the chicken whole when storing to retain juiciness, and include any tray juices for extra flavour and moisture. Allow the chicken to cool completely, then store the whole thighs in airtight containers. Slice into bite-sized cubes only when serving for maximum flavour and moisture retention and add the pineapple chipotle sauce as needed throughout the week. ENJOY! BEST WAYS TO SERVE AND PAIR THIS PROTEIN This Honey Chipotle Chicken works extremely well in simple, flavourful combinations, for example: – With coconut lime rice and pineapple salsa – With crispy roasted potatoes and corn salad – In a tortilla wrap with crunchy slaw and pickles SAUCE PAIRING SUGGESTIONS This chicken also pairs well with: – Pineapple chipotle sauce (included in this recipe) – Lime yogurt sauce – Spicy mayo

mealprep::chipotle ground beef.name: chipotle Ground Beef
ingredients: Splash of Water (optional, if needed for moisture) Chipotle Sauce (enough for 8+ meals)
steps: Prepare the creamy chipotle sauce. In a blender, combine Greek yogurt, light mayo, chipotle paste, honey, lime juice, salt, garlic powder, and paprika. Blend until completely smooth, then bottle and refrigerate. This sauce keeps well for the entire week.
Heat a pan over high heat, melt the reduced fat butter, then add the ground beef. Cook for 6-8 minutes, breaking it apart, until browned and fully cooked.

Reduce the heat to medium. Add salt, garlic powder, onion powder, parsley, paprika, cumin, and chipotle paste. Mix thoroughly so the spices coat the beef evenly. If the mixture seems dry, add a small splash of water. Finish with a drizzle of honey and cook until the beef becomes juicy, flavourful, and lightly caramelised. Remove from heat. For meal prep, allow the beef to cool, then store it as is (do not press, pack, or shred). BEST WAYS TO SERVE AND PAIR THIS PROTEIN This Chipotle Ground Beef works extremely well in simple, Mexican-inspired combinations, for example: – With mexican rice or cilantro lime rice – In a warm tortilla wrap with lettuce and pickled onions – In a taco bowl with corn, black beans, and shredded lettuce – Over roasted potatoes with fresh salsa SAUCE PAIRING SUGGESTIONS This beef also pairs well with: – Creamy chipotle sauce (included in this recipe) – Lime yogurt drizzle (yogurt, lime, garlic, salt) – Salsa verde or simple blended green sauce

mealprep::taco ground beef.name: Taco Ground Beef
ingredients: 1 Tsp Oregano 1000g beef, you will use 2 of this spice mix. Beef
stores for the week) 0% Greek yogurt of choice) Light Mayo Chipotle Paste Buffalo Hot Sauce Honey Powder
steps: MAKE THE TACO SPICE MIX: Combine salt, black pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, cumin, paprika, chilli powder, and oregano. Double the amounts to create 2 servings for the beef.

MAKE THE CHIPOTLE: S P I CY S AU C E Blend the yogurt, mayo, chipotle paste, buffalo hot sauce, honey, salt, paprika, and garlic powder until smooth and creamy. Bottle and refrigerate for up to one week.

COOK THE TACO BEEF: Heat a pan over medium-high heat and melt the butter. Add the ground beef and cook for 8-10 minutes, breaking it apart until fully browned. Stir in the tomato paste and 2 servings of taco spice mix, then cook for another 3-4 minutes until juicy, seasoned, and lightly caramelised.
FINISH & STORE: Top the cooked beef with shredded mozzarella. Store the beef in glass containers and cool completely before refrigerating.

mealprep::chilli lime chicken.name: chilli Lime chicken
ingredients: 50g (1.7oz) Light Soy Sauce Verde Creamy Sauce
steps: PREP & MARINATE THE CHICKEN: Trim excess fat from the chicken thighs and place them in a large mixing bowl. Add salt, black pepper, minced garlic, chilli flakes, smoked paprika, honey, lime zest, lime juice, and light soy sauce. Mix thoroughly with your hands until the chicken is fully coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate for at least 30 minutes (longer gives deeper flavour).

MAKE THE AHI VERDE SAUCE: In a blender, combine Greek yogurt, light mayo, honey, deseeded green chilli, fresh coriander, salt, and pepper. Blend until completely smooth, then bottle and refrigerate for the week.

COOK THE CHICKEN: Spread the marinated chicken thighs evenly on a lined sheet pan, ensuring they sit in a single layer for even browning. Oven bake or air fry at 200°C / 400°F for 20-22 minutes, until juicy with lightly charred edges.

REST & STORE THE CHICKEN: Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes to retain its juices. For meal prep, store the chicken whole in airtight glass containers and include all resting juices for maximum flavour and moisture. Slice into cubes only when serving.

mealprep::honey chilli crisp chicken.name: Honey chilli crisp chicken
ingredients: 1500g (53oz / 3.3lbs) Raw Boneless Chicken Thighs, trim excess fat
80g (2.1oz) Chilli Crisp Oil, drained well to keep mostly the crisp
steps: PREP & MARINATE THE CHICKEN: Trim any excess fat from the boneless chicken thighs, then place them in a mixing bowl. Add salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika, chilli crisp (mostly the crisp, minimal oil), and honey. Mix thoroughly with your hands until the chicken is evenly coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate for at least 30 minutes.

COOK THE CHICKEN: Spread the marinated chicken thighs evenly on a lined sheet pan. Oven bake or air fry at 190°C / 385°F for 16-18 minutes, until golden brown, juicy, and lightly charred on the edges.

REST & STORE THE: C H I C K E N ( K E E P T H I G H S W H O L E ) Once the chicken is cooked, allow it to rest for 5 minutes. For meal prep purposes, keep the chicken whole to retain maximum juiciness throughout the week. Pour all the tray juices over the thighs - this adds flavour, prevents drying, and enhances the honey chilli glaze as it cools. Slice into cubes only when serving to maintain the best texture and moisture. Enjoy your flavour packed, high protein chicken for the week!

mealprep::korean ground beef.name: Korean Ground Beef
steps: MAKE THE KOREAN MARINADE: In a mixing bowl, combine light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, and gochujang. Mix until smooth and fully combined. This marinade will be used for both the beef and optional rice.

COOK THE BEEF: Heat a pan over medium-high heat. Add a small amount of olive oil if needed, then cook the 1000g minced beef for about 6-8 minutes, breaking it apart with a spatula until fully browned with no pink remaining.

ADD THE MARINADE & STORE: Lower the heat to medium, then add half of the prepared Korean sauce, the chopped green onions, and sesame seeds. Stir well and cook for 4-5 minutes until the beef becomes glossy, golden, and slightly caramelised. Once done, remove from heat and transfer the cooked beef into airtight glass containers, spreading it evenly so it cools quickly before storing for meal prep.

mealprep::garlic herb chicken.name: Garlic Herb chicken
steps: MARINATE THE CHICKEN: Place the chicken thighs in a large mixing bowl. Add garlic paste, salt, black pepper, mixed herbs, parsley, paprika, chilli flakes, and olive oil. Mix thoroughly until every piece is coated and the colour deepens. Set aside to marinate.

COOK UNTIL GOLDEN: Heat a pan over medium heat and melt the butter. Add the chicken thighs in a single layer and cook for 5-6 minutes per side until a deep golden crust forms and the chicken is cooked through. Remove and set aside to rest, then repeat with the remaining chicken.

MEAL PREP STORAGE: Keep the chicken whole for storing (this prevents moisture loss). Let it rest fully, then transfer the thighs to airtight glass containers along with the buttery pan juices for flavour. Slice into cubes only when serving to keep each portion juicy and tender. ENJOY!

mealprep::teriyaki ground beef.name: Teriyaki Ground Beef
ingredients: 45g (1.6oz) Light Butter (for cooking) Yum Yum Sauce
Dash of Milk for desired consistency
steps: MAKE THE TERIYAKI SAUCE BASE: In a bowl, mix together light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, honey/maple syrup, rice vinegar, and minced garlic. This will be used to flavour the beef once browned.
BROWN THE BEEF: Heat a pan over medium-high heat and melt the light butter. Add the ground beef and cook for 6-8 minutes, breaking it apart with a spatula until fully browned with no pink remaining.

ADD THE FLAVOUR: Lower the heat to medium. Add all of the prepared teriyaki sauce, plus green onions and sesame seeds. Stir well and cook for another 4-5 minutes until the beef becomes golden, juicy, and slightly sticky. Transfer to airtight glass containers and allow to cool before storing.

mealprep::nacho ground beef.name: Nacho Ground Beef
ingredients: 1/2-1 Tsp Chilli Powder (adjust heat to preference) 1400g beef, you will use 3 of this spice mix.
steps: COOK THE BEEF: Heat a large pan over medium high heat, add the butter, then cook the ground beef for 5-7 minutes, breaking it apart with a spatula until fully browned with no pink remaining.

ADD SEASONINGS: Once browned, stir in the taco spice mix, tomato paste, chopped jalapeños, and jalapeño juice. Cook for 2 more minutes until the flavours intensify and the beef turns juicy and fragrant. Set aside.

MAKE THE NACHO CHEESE SAUCE: Using the same pan on low heat, add the evaporated milk, American cheese slices, cheddar, and a splash of jalapeño juice (optional). Stir continuously until the cheese melts and the sauce becomes silky and smooth. Transfer to a container and refrigerate for later use.

mealprep::beef shawarma.name: Beef Shawarma
ingredients: 1 Tsp Ginger Powder will use 3.5 servings for 1000g beef.
steps: MARINATE THE BEEF: Thinly slice the sirloin beef into extra thin strips. Place in a large mixing bowl and add 2.5 servings of shawarma spice mix, olive oil, and lemon juice. Mix thoroughly until fully coated and set aside to marinate.
PREPARE THE ONION MIX: In a separate bowl, combine the thinly sliced red onion, chopped parsley, sumac, and lemon juice. Mix well and set aside. This will be used as a fresh topping.

COOK THE BEEF: Heat a pan on high heat and melt some of the butter. Sear the marinated beef for 1-2 minutes to get a quick char. Reduce heat to medium, add the remaining butter and parsley, and sauté for another 1-2 minutes until evenly coated and golden brown. Remove the beef from the pan and set aside. Repeat until all beef is cooked. STORAGE, FREEZING & REHEATING GUIDE (For All Chicken & Ground Beef Meal Prep Recipes) This master guide applies to every protein recipe in this section - including chicken thighs, chicken breast, and ground/minced beef. The goal: keep your proteins juicy, tender, and flavourful all week. HOW TO KEEP PROTEINS JUICY ALL WEEK  Do not slice chicken until serving.  Always include leftover juices/glaze with chicken.  Store in shallow containers for faster cooling.  Avoid overcooking - reheating always reduces moisture slightly.  Air fryer is the best tool to revive texture.  Only reheat the portion you plan to eat - avoid reheating multiple times. STORAGE - FRIDGE (4-5 DAYS) Chicken (Whole Thighs or Cubed Thigh Meat)  Allow chicken to cool completely before storing.  Store whole thighs whenever possible - this preserves juiciness better than sliced or cubed chicken.  Add sheet pan juices, glaze, or cooking sauce to the container for moisture and flavour (do not over-saturate).  Store in airtight glass containers for maximum freshness. Ground / Minced Beef  Allow beef to cool fully before storing.  Store in shallow airtight containers to prevent moisture buildup.  Store all sauces separately.  Seasoned or tomato-based beef will continue to develop flavour over 24 hours - this is normal and enhances taste. FREEZING (UP TO 2-3 MONTHS) Chicken  Freeze cooked chicken whole for best texture.  Add a small amount of glaze/sauce to prevent drying (avoid flooding the container).  Freeze in airtight containers or freezer bags.  Defrost overnight in the fridge before reheating. Ground / Minced Beef  Freeze in flat portions (bags or thin containers) for faster thawing.  Add just enough pan juices to prevent dryness.  Do not freeze yogurt based, mayo- based, or citrus-heavy sauces - store those fresh only. REHEATING - CHICKEN (BEST METHODS) Aim to heat through while keeping the chicken juicy, with optional crisp edges. Air Fryer (Best Overall) 190°C / 375°F  Whole thighs: 5-7 minutes  Cubed chicken: 4-5 minutes Oven 180°C / 350°F  8-10 minutes (cover with foil for juiciness)  Uncover for final 1-2 minutes for crisp edges Pan Method Medium heat  Add 1-2 tbsp water, broth, or sauce  Cover to steam, uncover to re-caramelise Microwave (Fastest) 30-45 seconds  Add a splash of liquid and cover  For crisp recipes, finish 1-2 minutes in air fryer REHEATING - GROUND BEEF Pan (Best Method) Medium heat  Add 1-2 tbsp water or broth  Stir 2-4 minutes until hot and revived Microwave  30-60 seconds depending on portion  Add a splash of water or lime juice  Cover to prevent drying Oven 180°C / 350°F  6-8 minutes  Add moisture before heating Now that you understand exactly how to cook, store, and reheat your proteins, you have everything you need to power through your weekly meal prep with confidence. With these techniques, your routine becomes faster, easier, and far more consistent - allowing you to enjoy delicious, high-protein meals all week without the stress or guesswork.
